GARRETT, JAMES HUGH ELIOT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Reading Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 25th Nov., 1887, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1894; mag. and collr., Dec., 1898.

GARRETT, ROBERT VERNON, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Berar Commn.—Entered the service, 11th Feb., 1875; regimental duty with 54th Foot till Jan., 1879; entered staff corps, Jan., 1879; with 5th and 4th Infantry, Hyderabad contingent to June, 1887; asst. commr., June, 1887; special asst. commr., June, 1895; dep. commr., May, 1896; ret., Nov., 1904.

GARSTIN, NORMAN ANTHONY, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1857, as dep. collr. and mag., Bengal; transf. to Oudh, Sept., 1859, as extra asst. commr.; asst. district supt. of police, Dec., 1863; district supt., Jan., 1867; ret., Dec., 1893.

GARTHWAITE, LISTON, B.A., late Educn. Dept., Madras.—Served from 8th April, 1857, as head master of various schs., and as dep. inspr. of schs.; from Sept., 1869, served as inspr. of schs.; fellow of Madras univ., March, 1884; compiled Kanarese and Malayalam arzis for the sec. of state, and also various textbooks; ret., Dec., 1888; K.-i-H. medal, May, 1900.

GARWOOD, JOHN FREDERICK, Col., R.E., late Indian Mil. Works Services.—First commn., 17th April, 1866; arrived, 17th Feb., 1872, and served in the public works dept. in the N.W. Prov.; suptg. engr., Sept., 1890; on field service in the Zohb valley, 1890; acted as suptg. engr., Baluchistan civil works, and public works sec. to agent, in 1886, 1890, and 1892; transf. to Bombay command, March, 1895; and to head-quarters as dep. dir., April, 1895; suptg. engr., mil. works dept., Madras, March, 1897; chief engr., March, 1899; ret., April, 1901.

GASKELL, WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (under sec. to govt., United Prov.).—Educ. at Loughborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1904.

GASKIN, LIONEL EDWARD PALMER, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

GASS, HORACE ARCHIBALD, Forest Dept.,

Madras (conservator).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1875; dep. consr., Feb., 1876; consr., July, 1902.

GATES, FRANK CAMPBELL, Barr. (1891), Indian C.S. (chief sec. to govt., Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 13th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1887; under sec., Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; junr. sec., March, 1891; sec., March, 1893; rev. sec., Jan., 1895; sec. to govt., May, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901; chief sec. to govt., Sept., 1903; member of legisl. council, Burma, Sept., 1903.

GATHERER, ARTHUR BRANDER, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Arrived, 8th Nov., 1864, and served in the public works dept., Madras and Mysore; exec. engr., Sept., 1873; asst. sec. to rest., public works dept., Haidarabad, Dec., 1885; suptg. engr., March, 1889; transf. to Burma, April, 1889; chief engr. and sec. to govt. pub. works dept., Mar., 1900; ret., Aug., 1900.

GAULD, WILLIAM, India Office (second-class clerk, Public Works Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 28th Dec., 1895; transf. to public works dept., Jan., 1902.

GAUNTLETT, MAGER FREDERIC, Indian C.S., Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acct.-gen., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 27th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. officer; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1897; dep. acct.-gen., N.W.P. and Oudh, March, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1901; transf. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. acct.-gen., United Prov., April, 1903.

GAY, EDWARD, M.A., late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. asst. in the fin. dept., Sept., 1865; asst. compt.-gen. of accts., June, 1867; acting dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1868; dep. compt.-gen., Calcutta, Sept., 1869; acting acctnt.-gen., Bengal, 1874-75; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, and commr. of paper currency, April, 1879; compt. and auditor-gen. and head commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1888; ret., Nov., 1891.

GAYATONDE, G. V. (see Gopal Vishwambhar Gayatonde).

GAYER, ARTHUR HENRY.—Entd. the service as insp. of police, Aug., 1886; asst. dist. supt. of police, Jan., 1888; persl. asst. to chief commr., Burma, Jan., 1891; asst. commr., Sept., 1891; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., Bengal, Feb., 1896; supt. of stamps and stationery, March, 1899, to March, 1901.

GEAKE, EDWARD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1834; arrived, 25th Nov., 1836, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., April, 1893; mag. and collr., June, 1897.

GEIDT, BERNARD GEORGE, Indian C.S. (judge, high court, Calcutta).—Educ. at Shrewsbury; apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 30th Nov., 1876, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and in Assam as asst. commr. and personal asst. to the chief commr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1894; member, lieut.-gov.'s council,

Bengal, June, 1901; offg. judge, high court, Calcutta, April and Nov., 1902 and Jan., 1904; confd., May, 1904.

GELL, HERBERT GEORGE, Police Dept., Bombay (commr. of police).—Joined the service April, 1876, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Sept. 1877; dep. commr., Nov., 1881; dist. supt., Mar., 1899; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, Jan., 1902; confd., Jan., 1904.

GEOGHEGAN, HENRY THOMAS, B.A., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the dept., Nov., 1868, as exec. engr., Punjab N. state railway; suptg. engr., Indus valley railway, May, 1877; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India for state railways, Feb., 1879; engr.-in-chief, Bhopal railway, June, 1879; and Madras state railway surveys, Feb., 1885; ret'd., 1890.

GEORGE, DUNCAN, A.M.I.C.E., Bombay, Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., March, 1878; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

GEORGE, EDWARD CLAUDIUS SCOTNEY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dulwich and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 7th March, 1887, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; dep. commr., May, 1898; on special duty with the Burma-China Boundary commn., Dec., 1897, to May, 1898, and from Nov., 1898, to July, 1899.

GERRARD, ARTHUR STAPLES, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 2nd Aug., 1873, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., July, 1881; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India for railways, Lucknow, 1892-93; ret'd., May, 1899.

GHAFUR KHAN, A. (see Abdul Ghafur Khan).

GHARPUREY, H. G. (see Hari Ganpat Rao Gharpurey).

GHAZANFURALI KHAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 17th Nov., 1899, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

GHOSAL, J. (see Jyotsnanath Ghosal).

GHOSE, MAHMOHUN, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras)—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Bombay, St. Peter's Coll., Exmouth, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 11th Feb., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Feb., 1899.

GHOSE, M. C. (see Mohim Chandra Ghose).

GHOSE, PREONATH, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. to the dept. as apprentice engr., Oct. 1873; asst. engr., April, 1876; exec. engr., Nov., 1888; ret'd. Nov., 1901.

GHOSE, S. K. (see Sarat Kumar Ghose).

GHOSE, T. K. (see Tarini Kumar Ghose).

GIBB, MALCOLM COUPER, LL.B., Indian C.S. (senr. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Harrow, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 11th Dec., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Nov., 1890; 1st asst., Feb., 1893; junr. collr. and mag., May, 1897; senr. collr. and dist. regtr., Aug., 1900; ditto and pol. agent, Savanur, May, 1902.

GIBBONS, JAMES BARRY, Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Bengal).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1881; from Aug., 1884, acted as civil surgn.; rest. physician med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Feb., 1885; sup. Campbell med. sch. and hosp., Sealdah, and prof. of med. jurisprudence, Calcutta med. coll., Oct., 1893; civil surgn., Bengal, June, 1903; author of a Manual of Medical Jurisprudence for India.

GIBBS, HENRY METCALFE, Bombay Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1876, and served as asst. inspector and inspector of cotton; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1883; supt., Nov., 1885; dist. supt., March, 1899.

GIBSON, BASIL THEODORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GIBSON, EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at St. Alban's Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 25th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine duty in Karnul, 1876-78; acting under sec. to govt., Feb., 1880; private sec. to govr. of Madras, Dec., 1880; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1882; sec. to the board of rev., Jan., 1884; collr. and mag., Sept., 1888; member of board of rev., Nov., 1893; member of legisl. council, Jan., 1894; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1896.

GIBSON, F. M. (med. offr., plague research laboratory, Bombay).—Joined the service, 8th October, 1897, and was placed on plague duty under Bombay govt.; med. offr., plague research laboratory, Jan., 1901.

GIBSON, HENRY WILLIAM, Barr. (1870), late Oudh, Commn.—Volunteer in the Meerut light horse, 1857-58; served subsequently in the mil. police, 1858-61; asst. district supt. of police, Oudh, June, 1861; district supt., April, 1863; from July, 1864, served in the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; acted as supt. of encumbered estates, Sitapur, 1874; dep. commr., Nov., 1877; ret'd., April, 1894.

GIBSON, J. R., late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1862; arrived, Sept., 1860, and served as asst. supt., asst. settl. officer, and dep. supt.; from 1881 to 1885 served in Egypt, being attached to the intelligence dept. during the campaign of 1882 (medal and Khedive's star); supt., rev. survey, Ratnagiri, May, 1885; from 1885 to 1890, was again employed under the Egyptian govt., and received the order of the Osmanieh; resumed his post in May, 1890, but returned immediately to Egypt; ret'd. from the dept., June, 1892; recd. Grand Cordon of "Mejidieh," 1900.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

GIDUMAL, D. (see Dayaram Gidumal).

GIFFARD, GERALD GODFRAY, Major, I.M.S. (prof. med. coll., Madras).—First commn., 31st March, 1890; in mil. employ till Dec., 1897, when apptd. resdt. surg., gen. hosp., and prof. of pathology, Madras; dist. med. and sany. offr., Chingleput, Nov., 1899; 3rd physn., gen. hosp., and prof. of materia medica, med. coll.,

Madras, July, 1901; first surgn., gen. hosp. and profrr. of surgery, med. coll., May, 1903.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1875, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Oct., 1886; transf. to Burma, Feb., 1887.

GILBERT-COOPER, BRYAN GARDINER, Survey of India (dep. supt.).—Joined the service, Jan., 1878; asst. settl. offrr., March, 1879; asst. supt., Aug., 1880; dep. supt., Dec. 1895.

GILES, ARTHUR HENRY, late Police Dept., Bengal.—Joined the dept. as asst. supt., 14th Feb., 1863; district supt., Jan., 1866; dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1890; served temporarily under the govt. of Ceylon in 1888; has publ. report on police of Bombay, Madras and Calcutta, 1887; and report on administration of Police, Ceylon, 1889; ret'd., April, 1897.

GILES, EDWARD, C.I.E., M.A., Bombay Educl. Dept. (dir. of public instruction).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; arrived, 14th May, 1873, and served as profrr. of history and pol. economy, Elphinstone coll., Bombay; eduel. inspr., May, 1875; dir. of public instruction, April, 1897; adml. member of legisl. council, March, 1898, Jan., 1901, and May, 1904; C.I.E., Jan., 1903.

GILES, FRANK, late Indian C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 26th Oct., 1873, and served in the N.W. Prov., as asst. mag. and collr. and joint mag.; under sec. to govt., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1886; offg. sec. to board of rev., Sept., 1887; senr. asst. commr., May, 1888; offg. commr., Kumaun, 1889, 1891, and 1893; dep. commr., Oct., 1891; acted as legal remembrancer in 1893; member and sec. of provl. legisl. council, Nov., 1893, to Jan., 1894; supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1896; ret'd., July, 1898.

GILES, ROBERT, C.I.E., M.A., late Sind Commn.—Arrived 8th Dec., 1868; asst. settl. officer, Indus survey, March, 1869; served from Jan., 1870, as dep. collr., supt. of police, and eduel. inspr. in Sind; 2nd asst. collr. in Sind, July, 1884; dep. commr., Dec., 1885; coll. and mag., Nov., 1895; member of plague committee, Karachi, March, 1897; acting commr., Oct., 1897; confd., Dec., 1900; C.I.E., June, 1899; member, plague committee, Jan., 1900; ret'd., April, 1902.

GILL, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 23rd Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1887; asst. commr., Nov., 1889; settl. officer, March, 1890; joint mag., July, 1890; district and sessions judge, March, 1893; offg. legal remembrancer, April, 1897; confd., April, 1902; member, legisl. council, Nov., 1903; ret'd., Dec., 1904; author of 'Gazetteer of the Ghazipur District.'

GILLAN, Rev. G. G., M.A., late Ecclesi. Dept., Bengal.—Asst. chaplain of the Church of Scotland, 4th Jan. 1866; chaplain, Jan., 1876; senr. chaplain of the church of Scotland in Bengal, and of St. Andrew's church, March, 1880; ret'd., 1889.

GILLAN, ROBERT WOODBURN, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1889), Indian C.S. (sec., board of rev.,

United Prov.).—Educ. at Ayr Acad. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; offg. city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894; asst. commr., May, 1896; settl. officer, Oct., 1896; dep. commr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of rev., April, 1902; sec., board of rev., Jan. 1903.

GILLESPIE, JAMES THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. registrar, high court, Madras).—Educ. at Newry Intermediate Sch., the Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 21st Dec., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. registrar, high court, Jan., 1897.

GILLMAN, HERBERT FRANCIS WEBB, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Nov., 1895; collr., Vizianagram Zemindari, Aug., 1893.

GIMLETTE, GEORGE HART DESMOND, C.I.E., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (resy. surgn., Hyderabad).—First commn., 31st March, 1879; on mil. duty till July, 1883; on special cholera work in Egypt, Aug. to Oct., 1883; offg. residency surgn., Nepal, Nov., 1883; agency surgn., Baghelkhand, March, 1891; residency surgn., Indore, Dec., 1897; ditto, Hyderabad, May, 1901; C.I.E., Nov., 1901.

GIRARD, ROBERT GEORGE (collr. of income-tax, Calcutta).—Joined the service 1st Jan., 1876; held non-gazetted appts. till 7th April, 1896, when apptd. to be offg. collr. of income-tax, Calcutta; confd., June, 1895.

GIRINDRA NARAIN DEB, KUMAR, Statutory C.S. (joint mag and dep. collr., Bengal).—Offg. dep. mag. and dep. collr., Bardwan, Aug., 1878; apptd. under the native civil service rules proby. asst. mag. and collr., Dec., 1880; confd., 10th Feb., 1886; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892.

GLADSTONE, CECIL ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 13th Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. commr.; on special duty at Delhi at the imperial assemblage, Dec., 1876; transf. to Burma as dep. commr., Sept., 1886; offd. as commr., April, 1889; retransfd. to the Punjab, as dep. commr., Dec., 1889; ret'd., Sept., 1894.

GLANCY, REGINALD ISIDORE ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901 to Sept., 1903; settlmt. offrr., Bannu, Oct., 1903.

GLASS, JAMES GEORGE HENRY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—2nd asst. engr., Central Prov., 27th June, 1867; exec. engr., April, 1871; sec. to chief commr., public works dept., Central Prov., July, 1885; suptg. engr. and sec., Aug., 1888; C.I.E., May, 1890; chief engr. and sec., Jan.-March, 1893; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, buildings and roads branch, March, 1894; transf. to Bengal in similar capacity, Jan., 1896; mem-

ber of lieut.-govr.'s council; retd., Mar., 1898; member of board of visitors, R.I.E. Coll., July, 1901.

GLAZIER, EDWARD GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at N. London Coll. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 25th Sept., 1861; from April, 1862, served in Bengal rev. and jud. depts., and was apptd. mag. and collr., 1st grade, Oct., 1879; retd., 1891; author of 'Report on the district of Rungpore,' 'Further Notes on the Rungpore Records,' 'Report on the Famine in Rungpore.'

GLEADOW, FRANK, Forest Dept., Bombay (conservator.)—Apptd. asst. consr., 4th Dec., 1879; dep. consr., July, 1887; prof. of forestry, coll of sci., Poona, May, 1892; dep. dir., forest sch., Dehra Dun., June, 1896; conservator, Dec., 1903.

GLENNY, WILLIAM HENRY, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Newry Sch., Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; collr. and mag., Karnul, April, 1880; acting dir. of rev. settl. and agric., Oct., 1885; collr. and mag., N. Arcot, May, 1886; retd., 1888.

GLOSTER, JOHN CLERKE, Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay)—Educ. at Middleton Coll., Cork, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 15th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Jan., 1897; 1st asst., Jan., 1901; judge and sessions judge, Dec., 1902.

GODBOLE, K. R. (see Kashinath Ramchandra Godbole).

GODFREY, CHARLES WILLIS, Col., Indian Army, late Rev. Survey Dept., Bombay.—Educ. at foreign schs.; arrived, 9th Sept., 1858, and served in the Bombay artillery; from Aug., 1864, served in the dept. as asst. supt., and was promoted to supt. of survey, Jan., 1885.

GODFREY, FRANCIS EDWARD, late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. comptr., and posted to Bombay, 1st April, 1871; dep. examr., Jan., 1880; examr., Jan., 1882; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen. for inspn. duty, Dec., 1892; examr. of telegraph accts., April, 1894; examr. of pub. wks. accts., Bengal, Aug., 1897; retd., April, 1903.

GODFREY, GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 20th Oct., 1873, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, May, 1874; served in Assam, 1874-80, and again from Oct., 1883; dep. commr., Jan., 1885; acting inspr.-gen. of police, &c., April, 1892; judge and commr., Assam valley districts, Oct., 1892; retd., Dec., 1898.

GODFREY, STUART HILL, Major, Indian Army Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral)—In mil. employ from 22nd Jan., 1881; pol. asst., May, 1892; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Feb., 1900; pol. agent, and ditto, April, 1900; pol. agent in Zohb, Mar., 1901; pol. agent for Dir. Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1902; author of a translation of Pushkin's Kapetanskaya Dochka, a report on Gilgit agency, &c.

GODLEY, SIR ARTHUR, K.C.B., India Office (under sec. of State for India)—Educ. at Rugby, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; Hertford scholar, 1868; Ireland scholar, 1870; Eldon law scholar, 1874; called to the bar at Lincoln's Inn, 1876;

private sec. to the first lord of the treasury, 1872-74, and again, 1880-82; commr. of inland rev., 1882-83; C.B., 1882; permanent under sec. of state for India, 30th Sept., 1883; member of the Indian currency committee, 1892-93; K.C.B., Jan., 1893.

GODREZJI DORABJI PUDUMJI, B.A. Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. acctnt.-gen. Bombay)—Joined the service April, 1877, and served as asst. acct.-gen. at Allahabad, Bombay, Rangoon and Calcutta; transf. to Madras Feb., 1896; dep. acct.-gen., Punjab, May, 1898; dep. acct.-gen., Bombay, Dec., 1899; offg. comptr., Hyderabad, Mar., 1903, offg. compt.-gen., Nov., 1903; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, April, 1904; ditto, Bombay, June, 1904.

GODSELL, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late India Office.—Junr. asst., India audit office, 30th Sept., 1860; junr. inspr., Dec., 1868; asst. to the auditor, Oct., 1892; auditor, Jan., 1895; Kt., Jan., 1903; retd., Feb., 1903.

GODWIN, CHARLES, India Office (2nd class clerk, store depôt)—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depôt, 2nd May, 1904.

GODWIN-AUSTEN, HAROLD, Berar, Commn. (dep. commr.)—Educ. at the Royal Acad., Gosport; joined the service, 27th Feb., 1877, and held offg. appts. at Port Blair and in the Nicobars; on special duty with the joint commr. of Ladakh in 1880; extra asst. supt., Port Blair, Feb., 1883; transf. to Berar as asst. commr., Nov., 1889; special asst. commr., June, 1893; dep. commr., June, 1895.

GOKAL CHAND BADHWAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1898), Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.)—Educ. at the Punjab Univ., and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived 18th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

GOLDINGHAM, JOHN DALRYMPLE, late Madras C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1853; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and sub-judge; judge of small causes, July, 1862; civil and sessions judge, Oct., 1868; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1872; retd., Sept., 1888.

GOLDNEY, WILLIAM GEORGE, Punjab Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-gen.)—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1873; asst. dist. supt., Oct., 1875; dist. supt., June, 1884, dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1903.

GOLDSMID, FREDERIC LESTOCK, late Bombay Police Dept.—Entered the service, 1st March, 1872, as asst. supt., rev. survey; dep. collr., March, 1876; asst. supt. of police, June, 1881; supt., Jan., 1883; 1st grade, Nov., 1900; retd., Oct., 1903.

GOLDSMITH, SEPTIMUS JESSE, Surgn.-Major, late Indian Medl. Service.—Joined the Bombay med. service, April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873; served as civil surgn. in N. Kanara and Gujarat; agency surgn., Bhaghelkhand, Sept., 1875; also tutor to Maharaja of Rewah from Oct., 1883; afterwards in med. charge of Bhaghelkhand agency and dispensaries; retd., March, 1891.

GOMPERTZ, HENRY JAMES COLLEY, late Madras Rev. Survey Dept.—Apptd., 10th Aug., 1859; dep. supt., 1st class, July, 1887; employed in 1888 on special duty on boundary questions, Pudukottai, and in 1889, Karikal and Tanjore; retd., Jan., 1893.

GOMPERTZ, RICHARD LOUIS DALZIEL, late India Telegraph Dept.—Apptd. asst. supt., 9th

Nov., 1870, and employed on various lines in Bombay, Bengal, Madras, &c.; supt., Oct., 1887; chief supt., Jan., 1899; director, construction branch, Aug., 1901; ret'd., July, 1904.

GOOD, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrig. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Jan., 1879; supt. engr., Feb., 1895; ret'd., April, 1897.

GOODE, SAMUEL WALTER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Way Coll., Adelaide, Adelaide Univ., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 12th Jan., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GOODEVE, ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 24th Nov., 1886, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; asst. compt. India treasuries, June, 1893; dep. accont.-gen., Bombay, May, 1894; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1901.

GOODFELLOW, ARTHUR TRETHOWAN, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at the Royal Indian Engrg. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Oct., 1894, and posted to N.W. Prov. and Oudh; examiner, May, 1886; ret'd., Nov., 1901.

GOODFELLOW, CHARLES AUGUSTUS, V.C. Lieut.-Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Bombay.—Joined Addiscombe, July, 1853; 2nd lieut., June, 1855; arrived, March, 1857; served with the Central India field force, 1857-58 (medal and clasp, V.C.), and with the Okhamandal and Kathiawar field force, 1859; joined the dept. as special asst. engr., harbour defences, Bombay, April, 1860; exec. engr., Feb., 1862; served in the Abyssinian expdn., 1867-68 (medal); suptg. engr., May, 1884; chief engr. for irrig., Sept., 1887; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1891.

GOODRICH, HARRY ST. AUBYN, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., dep. dir. of rev. settlt., and sub-collr. and joint mag.; collr. and mag., March, 1882; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1884-85, and 1885-86; ret'd., Feb., 1890.

GOODRICK, G. M., late Bengal Customs Dept.—Served from 30th Nov., 1859, as preventive officer and appraiser, Calcutta custom house, and was apptd. asst. collr. of customs, July, 1871, and dep. collr. of Calcutta, March, 1880; also supt. of excise rev., collr. of stamp rev., and a collr. under the licence tax act; ret'd., 1880.

GOODRIDGE, WALTER SOMERVILLE, C.I.E., Capt., R.N.—Dir. of the Royal Indian Marine, 5th March, 1898 to March, 1904; C.I.E., Jan., 1901.

GOODWIN, FRANK, India Public Works Dept. (locomotive supt.).—Apptd. to the service as asst. locomotive supt., May, 1879; locomotive supt., Nov., 1890.

GOOROO DAS BANERJEE (see Banerjee, G. D.).

GOPAL CHANDRA RAY, Finl. Dept., Govt. of India (dep. accont.-gen., Bengal).—Entered the dept., 8th June, 1891, as asst. accont.-gen., Bengal; asst. compt. -gen., March, 1892; dep. accont.-gen., Madras, March, 1897; asst. accont.-gen.

N.W. P. and O., June, 1897; asst. accont.-gen., Bengal, May, 1898, and April, 1900; ditto, Madras, July, 1899; dep. accont.-gen., Bengal, April, 1902; on depn. in office of admr.-gen. from May, 1902; again dep. accont.-gen., Bengal, June, 1903.

GOPAL DAS, Rai Bahadur, late Punjab Commn.—Served in the settlt. dept. and subord. jud. dept. of the Punjab from 6th July, 1851; extra jud. asst., Oct., 1888; author of a 'Manual for preparation of Records of Rights' and of histories of the Gujranwala and Peshawur districts; ret'd., 1889.

GOPAL VISHWAMBHAR GAYATONDE, L.C.E., Bombay Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service 15th March, 1880, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Dec., 1895.

GOPALA CHARIYAR, SARUKKAI, B.A., B.L., Diwan Bahadur (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Apptd. sub. judge, Madras, 21st March, 1885; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1900; confid., June, 1903.

GOPALA KRISHNA AIYAR, S., Rai Bahadur, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Asst. engr., Sept., 1880; exec. engr., Feb., 1894.

GOPENDRA KRISHNA DEB, KUMAR, M.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Served in the subord. exec. service, Bengal, from 27th Jan., 1876, and offd. as registrar of Calcutta and inspr. of registrn. offices; apptd. under the native civil service rules, Nov., 1882, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1891; inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1895.

GORDON, ALEXANDER EVANS, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 18th April, 1865; from May, 1872, served in Bengal as asst. commr. on special duty with the Maharaja of Johore, Dec., 1875; captonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Dum-Dum, March, 1876; tutor to the Maharaja of Darbhanga, June, 1877; on depn. to Kuch Behar, Dec., 1883; dep. commr., March, 1887; acted as jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, in 1893; placed on unemployed superny. list, July, 1900.

GORDON, DONALD CLUNES, late India Public Works Dept.—Joined the dept. in 1868, as asst. compt. of accts., N.W. Prov.; served subsequently as dep. compt. in the Punjab; compt. July, 1874; examr. of guaranteed railway accts., Bombay, 1877-79, and afterwards in Madras; examr., public works accts., Madras, May, 1881; in same capacity in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh, March, 1887; and in the Punjab, March, 1891; ret'd., April, 1897.

GORDON, GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Radley Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1893; transf. to Bengal as joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1894; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1896.

GORDON, HAMILTON WINKUP, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Nov., 1864, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1870; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1881; offd. at various periods as judge of high court, Calcutta; ret'd., June, 1897.

GORDON, HUNTLY PRYSE, late Madras C.S.—Educ. at New Coll., London, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., small cause court judge, and asst. commr.; collr. and mag., Dec., 1878; retd., Oct., 1887.

GORDON, SIR JOHN JAMES HOOD, K.C.B., General, Indian Army (Member of the Council of India).—First commn., 21st Aug., 1849; entered Bengal Staff Corps, 29th May, 1861; has received medals for the Indian mutiny, Jowaki, Afghan and Burma campaigns; asst. mil. sec. for Indian affairs at Horse Guards, Oct., 1890; member of the Council of India, Jan., 1897; K.C.B., May, 1898; author of 'The Sikhs.'

GORDON, PHILIP JAMES, Major, Indian Army, Survey of India (supdt., forest surveys, Bengal).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1881; apptd. to Survey of India as asst., supt., Nov., 1890; dep. supt., Dec., 1895; supdt., forest surveys, Bengal, Oct., 1900.

GORDON, RAMSAY FREDERICK CLAYTON, Major, Indian Army (commdt., mil. police, Burma).—First commn., 23rd Aug., 1834; after mil. service acted as dep. inspr. gen. of mil. police, Burma, from April, 1901, to April, 1902, and again from Nov., 1902, with subs. appt. of commdt. of mil. police.

GORDON, WEBSTER BOYLE, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1879; exec. engr., Oct., 1892; sanitary engr. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh with rank of suptg. engr., April, 1899; sec., Irrigation Commn, 1901-03; C.I.E., Jan. 1904.

GORDON-LEITH, W.E., late Bengal Jud. Dept.—Joined the service May, 1893; asst. sec. Govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Oct., 1893; dep. supt. and rememberer of legal affairs, Sept., 1896; resgd., April, 1902.

GORE, ST. GEORGE CORBET, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late Indian Survey Dept.—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1870; arrived, 15th Nov., 1872; from March, 1873, served on the great trigon. survey of India; on service in Afghanistan, 1879-80, with Sir D. Stewart's column on march from Kandahar to Kabul, as officer in charge of survey; attached to Afghan boundary commn., Aug., 1884; dep. supt., March, 1886; supt., trigon. surveys, Dehra Dun, May, 1894; surv. gen. of India, Oct., 1899; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; retd., May, 1904.

GORST, RIGHT HON. SIR JOHN ELDON, K.C., M.P.—Graduated at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, as 3rd wrangler, 1857; M.A., 1860; fellow of his coll.; called to the bar at the Inner Temple, 1865; Q.C., 1875; solicitor-gen. in Lord Salisbury's first administration, 1885-86; under sec. of state for India, in Lord Salisbury's second administration, Aug., 1886, to Nov., 1891; finl. sec. to the treasury, 1891-92; was British plenipotentiary at the labour conference at Berlin, 1890; lord rector of Glasgow univ., 1893; vice-prest. of committee of council on educn. in Lord Salisbury's third and fourth administrations July, 1895, to July, 1902; author of 'The Maori King.'

GOSS, LOUIS ALLAN, late Educn. Dept., Burma.—Held non-gazetted appts. from 5th Aug., 1874, to 7th April, 1878, when he was apptd. junr. inspr. of schs.; senr. inspr., March,

1893; in 1894, acted in addn. as dir. of public instrn.; retd. Oct., 1901; author of 'The Story of Wethan-da-ya.'

GOUDGE, JOSEPH ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (settlmt. offr., Bundelkhand States).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Taunton, London Univ., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. settlt. offr., Nov., 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1898; joint mag. and settlement officer, April, 1900; settlmt. offr., Bundelkhand States, Dec., 1903.

GOUGH, ARCHIBALD EDWARD, M.A., late Educ. Service, N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the dept., 2nd Nov., 1868, and served as profrr., Benares coll., and Muir coll., Allahabad; served in Bengal, 1878-86, as profrr. in the Hugli and presidency colls., and principal of the Calcutta Madrassa; principal, Muir coll., Allahabad, July, 1886; author of 'Papers relating to the Collection and Preservation of the Records of Ancient Sanskrit Literature in India,' 1878, &c., &c.; retd. July, 1895.

GOULDSBURY, CHARLES ELPHINSTONE, late Bengal Police Dept.—Joined the dept., 20th June, 1869, and served as asst. supt.; dist. supt., April, 1886; retd., Jan. 1903.

GOULDSBURY, DUDLEY ELPHINSTONE, Lieut.-Col., late Punjab Administration.—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1866; arrived, 1st March, 1867; served in mil. dept. to June, 1882, when he was transfed. to the Punjab as offg. cantonmt. mag.; re-transfd. to mil. dept., Oct., 1882; resumed his appt., May, 1883; confd. March, 1887; retd., Oct., 1898.

GOUMENT, CHARLES ERNEST VEAR, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll., Roorkee; apptd. apprentice engineer in the Punjab, Oct., 1879; asst. engr., July, 1880; exec. engr., May, 1893; under sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1899, and January to May, 1902; suptg. engr., Jan., 1904.

GOURLAY, WILLIAM ROBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAVKAR (see Madgavkar, G. D.).

GOWAN, HYDE CLARENDON, Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Prov.).—Educ. at Rugby and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 9th Dec., 1902, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

GOWERS, ERNEST ARTHUR, B.A., India Office (jun. clerk, judl. and public dept.).—Educ. at Rugby and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. upper division clerk, Inland Revenue, Dec., 1903; transfed. to India Office as junr. clerk, and attached to the judl. and public dept., 22nd Sept., 1904.

GRACE, CHARLES HALLYBURTON, Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., as ensign, Madras infantry, March, 1854; served in the Central Prov. from Sept., 1859, as asst. commr., dep. commr., and settlt. officer; acted as commr. of Jabalpur, 1882; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

GRACEY, HUGH KIRKWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (settlmt. offr., United Prov.).—Educ. at City of London Sch., London Univ., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 17th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; settlmt. offr., Cawnpore, Oct., 1903.

GRACEY, STEPHEN WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Trin. Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.

GRACEY, THOMAS, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Enniskillen Royal Sch.; 1st commn., 17th Dec., 1862; 1st asst. engr., N.W. Prov., Sept., 1867; exec. engr., Sept., 1870; engr.-in-chief, Pindi-Kohat section of Punjab N. railway, 1879; engr.-in-chief, provl. railways under construction, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Oct., 1882; sec. to chief commr., public works dept., Burma, April, 1887; suptg. engr., Feb., 1888; offg. consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Madras, July, 1890; consulting engr. to the govt. of India for railways, central divn., June, 1891; in similar capacity, Lucknow, June, 1892; chief engr., June, 1892; dir.-gen. of railways, and dep. sec., railway branch, Aug., 1895, C.S.I., June, 1897; sec. to govt. of India; railway branch, Nov., 1897; ret'd. Jan., 1900.

GRAHAM, GEORGE DASHWOOD, Bengal Police Dept. (dep. inspr.-genl. of police).—Joined the service 12th Aug., 1874, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., July, 1877; dist. supt., June, 1886; dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1902.

GRAHAM, GERALD EDWARD RUTLEDGE (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Ipswich Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 11th Dec., 1901, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

GRAHAM, JOHN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Rutland Coll. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

GRAHAM, JOHN FULLER, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Bruton Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 6th Dec., 1896, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and sub. divl. offr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Jan., 1902.

GRAHAM, ROBERT ARTHUR, Indian C.S. (sec. to commr. of salt, &c., rev., Madras).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 11th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1898; dep. commr., salt, abkari, and customs dept., Oct., 1900; sec. to commr. of salt, &c., rev., June, 1903.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FITZWILLIAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 15th Nov., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr., offg. under sec., and settlmt. offr.

GRAHAME, WILLIAM FRANCIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 4th

Jan., 1864, and served in the Madras rev. and jud. depts.; on special duty in 1868 with Dr. Janssen in connection with the solar eclipse; collr. and mag., June, 1882; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1882; ret'd. Jan., 1890.

GRANT, ALEXANDER, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., July, 1867, and employed in N.W. Prov. and Oudh on irrign. works; exec. engr., March, 1873; under sec. to govt., irrign. branch, in 1888; suptg. engr., Jan., 1893; ret'd., Oct., 1895.

GRANT, ALEXANDER, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 15th Oct., 1873, and posted to mil. works; transfed. to Punjab, Nov., 1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; transfed. to Burma, Aug., 1889; and to Assam, May, 1894; to Hyderabad, Oct., 1895; suptg. engr., May, 1902; ret'd., Sept., 1903.

GRANT, ALFRED HAMILTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 9th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to fin. commr., Jan., 1900; senr. sec., Nov., 1900; sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901 and Oct., 1903; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., July to Oct., 1903; with mission to Kabul, Nov., 1904.

GRANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

GRANT, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

GRANT, DONALD ST. JOHN DUNDAS, M.A., M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (profir., Lahore med. coll.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1881; served in the Punjab from Oct., 1886, as civil surgn.; chemical examr. to govt., and profir., Lahore med. coll., Sept., 1891; offg. in a similar capacity in Calcutta from April, 1903.

GRANT, GEORGE, Brigade-Surgn., M.B., late Med. Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Asst. surgn., Bengal med. establt., 10th Feb., 1850; arrived, 11th July, 1859; from June, 1860, served under the govt. of the N.W. Prov. as civil asst. surgn.; offg. supt., govt. press, and curator of govt. books, N.W. Prov., and chemical examr., Aug., 1866; supt. of new central prison, Farakhabad, Dec., 1866; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons and civil hosp., N.W. Prov., in 1877, 1879, and 1882; supt., Bareilly jails, Dec., 1877; dep. sanitary commr., 1st circle, Dec., 1883; ret'd., Oct., 1885.

GRANT, GEORGE FRANCIS MYTTON, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Brighton; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 12th Dec., 1864, and served as asst. collr. and mag., branch registrar-gen., and educt. inspr. in Sind; in charge of the Sachin state, May, 1870; junr. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Dec., 1882; junr. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Thana, March, 1885; senr. collr., Sept., 1886; commr., N. divn., April, 1890; ret'd., Nov., 1890.

GRANT, J. G. GARROW, Dr., late Emigration Dept., Bengal.—Served in the dept. from 22nd May, 1863, as med. inspr. and protector of

labourers, Kushtia; protector of emigrants and supt. of labour transports, Calcutta, Feb., 1871; supt. of emigration, Calcutta, Jan., 1874; retd., 1889.

GRANT, JOHN HAY, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury, 1856; arrived, 6th Feb., 1857, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., railway mag., and asst. rev. and police commr.; junr. collr., Sept., 1877; municipal commr., Bombay, Nov., 1877; collr. of Bombay, and supt. of stamps and stationery, Aug., 1883; special commr. for Bombay for international exhibn. at Calcutta, 1883; retd., Jan., 1888.

GRANT, JOHN PYNE, Col., I.S.C., late Rev. Survey of Mysore.—First commn., Bombay infantry, Jan., 1856; joined the rev. survey, 1860; supt., Jan., 1868; retd., Jan., 1890.

GRANT, THOMAS, India Office (2nd class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 28th May, 1875.

GRANT, TREVOR JOHN CHICHELE, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 27th Jan., 1858; served in Bengal as asst. and joint mag.; mag. and collr., May, 1874; offg. sec., board of rev., April, 1874; retd., Jan., 1893.

GRANT-BROWN, GEORGE EUSTACE RIQU, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 9th Dec., 1889, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Aug., 1901.

GRANT-DUFF, Rt. HON. SIR MOUNTSTUART ELPHINSTONE, G.C.S.I., C.I.E., F.R.S.—Born in 1829; educ. at Edinburgh, and at Balliol Coll., Oxford (M.A., 1853); called to the bar at the Inner Temple in 1854; represented the Elgin burghs in parliament from 1857 to 1881; under sec. of state for India, Dec., 1868, to Feb., 1874; under sec. of state for the colonies, April, 1880, to Aug., 1881; govr. of Madras, Nov., 1881, to Dec., 1886; was lord rector of the univ. of Aberdeen, 1866-72; King's trustee of British Museum, 1903; author of 'Studies in European Politics,' 'Elgin Speeches,' 'A Political Survey,' 'Memoir of Sir H. S. Maine,' 'Ernest Rénan,' 'Notes from a Diary,' &c.

GRANVILLE, HENRY CHUDLEIGH, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Civil Engrg. Coll.; entered the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1876 and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Dec., 1887. (India medal and three clasps, 1897-8).

GRAVES, SOMERSET HENRY PAUL, Col., Indian Army, late Burma Police Dept.—First commn., 2nd Sept., 1868; served in the 26th native infantry, and in June, 1885, was apptd. supt. of police, Port Blair; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Upper Burma, May, 1887; reverted to mil. duty, 1888.

GRAY, EVELYN, Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (collr. of land rev., customs, and opium, Bombay).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 27th Dec., 1878, and served as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; transfed. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Dec., 1891; dep. commr., Jan., 1892; re-transf. to Bombay as junr. collr., Dec., 1896; senr. collr., Jan., 1898; collr. of land rev., customs and opium, supdt. of stamps, &c., Jan., 1900.

GRAY, MALCOLM ALEXANDER, B.A., Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late dep. commr., Assam.—Ensign, 3rd (Buffs) regt., 7th Nov., 1868; arrived, 1st Feb., 1870; served in the Assam commn., from Aug., 1873, as offg. asst. commr. and asst. inspr. of immigrants; confd. as asst. commr., July, 1877; forest settl. officer, Oct., 1885; dep. commr., Sept., 1888; retd., April, 1891.

GRAY, PERCY, Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 30th Dec., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; junr. sec., board of rev., April-July, 1885; joint mag., Sept., 1887, and asst. commr., Feb., 1890; judge, small cause court, Allahabad, May, 1891; registrar, high court, March, 1892; dep. commr., March, 1894; mag. and coll., Nov., 1895; commr., Oct., 1902.

GRAY, ROBERT, M.B., Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the Indian med. dept. in 1859; arrived, 11th March, 1860, and served in the Punjab from Feb., 1863, as civil asst. surgn., jail supt. and surgn. at various stations; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Sept., 1884; retd., April, 1891.

GRAY, WELLINGTON, L.M., L. Ch., Brigade-Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Med. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the Indian med. service, 31st March, 1866; arrived, 9th Nov., 1866, and served as profir. and principal, Grant med. coll., surgn. at the Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., curator of Victoria and Albert museum, and sec. to the surgn.-gen.; retd., April, 1893.

GRAY, WILLIAM BORMAN, India Public Works Dept. (examiner).—Apptd. asst. examr., 12th April, 1876, and posted to Punjab Northern State Rly.; dep. examr., Jan., 1882; examr., Dec., 1886.

GREANY, JOHN PHILIP, M.D., Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service 31st March, 1875, and was employed on mil. duty, and in various acting civil posts; apptd. civil surgn., July, 1883; acted as dep. sanitary commr. in 1888 and 1893; offg. P.M.O. Aden dist., 1903.

GREAVES, SIR GEORGE RICHARDS, K.C.B., K.C.M.G., Gen.—First commn., 30th Nov., 1849; served in the Mutiny, the Yusufzai expdn. of 1858 (medal with clasp), and the war in New Zealand, 1862-66 (medal); chief of the staff in the Ashanti expdn. of 1873-74 (medal with clasp and C.B.), and in the Soudan campaign in 1885 (K.C.B., medal with clasp); commander-in-chief of Bombay army, and second member of council of govr. of Bombay, March, 1890; resigned, April, 1893; retd., from the army, June, 1896.

GREAVES, RICHARD HERBERT, late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 19th Jan., 1870, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; transfed. to Assam as district and sessions judge, Sylhet, Oct., 1887; retd., July, 1896.

GREEN, CHARLES ROBERT MORTIMER, Major, Indian Med. Service (civil surgn., Simla).—First commn., Sept., 1886; in mil. employ till Oct., 1891; civil surgn., Manbhum, July, 1893; ditto, Durbhanga, May, 1897; joint civil surgn., Simla, March, 1902.

GREEN, GEORGE EDWARD TEMPEST, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—Entered

the service, Feb., 1882; on mil. duty till Dec., 1885, when he joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1900.

GREEN, HENRY HENNIS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to Bengal; exec. engr.; Feb., 1896.

GREENE, WALTER JAMES, Finl. Dept., India Office (clerk for non-effective accts.).—Writer, 1st July, 1867; clerk, 2nd class, Aug., 1878; clerk for exam. of army non-effective accts., March, 1885.

GREENSHIELDS, ROBERT SCOTT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 9th Dec., 1879, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam, March, 1882, as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to the chief commr., March, 1886; offd. as dir. of land rev. and agric. in 1889; dep. commr., Nov., 1889; mag. and collr. Bengal, Nov., 1897.

GREENSTREET, WILLIAM LEES, Col., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Oct., 1864; arrived, Jan., 1870; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1871, as asst. engr., N.W. Prov.; transfed. to mil. works branch, 1872; exec. engr., Nov., 1874; suptg. engr., July, 1885; took part in the Hazara expdn., 1891; chiefengr., Sept., 1893; posted to Bombay command, April, 1895; returned to Europe, Jan., 1899; retd., Nov., 1900.

GREER, RICHARD TOWNSEND, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (chairman of the corporation of Calcutta).—Educ. at Erasmus Coll., Drogheeda, and Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 10th Dec., 1877, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and asst. sec. to chief commr.; transfed. to Bengal, Nov., 1888, as joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., March, 1893; insp.-gen. of police, March, 1900; chairman of the corporation of Calcutta, Oct., 1900; member, lieut. govr.'s council, Bengal, Jan., 1901; C.S.I., Jan. 1904.

GREER, ROBERT FRANCIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Roy. Acad. Inst., Belfast, and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

GREENEN, RICHARD, B.A., Barr. (1888), Indian C.S. (dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept.).—Educ. at Dulwich and at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 1st Dec., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1891; asst. supt. and small cause judge, Dehra Dun, 1892-93; acted as under sec. to govt. in 1894, and from July, 1895; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1895; dep. commr., April, 1897; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1898; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., April, 1900; confd. in that appt., March, 1904; author of 'Heroes Five.'

GREGG, WILLIAM HENRY, M.B., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—First commn., 1869; served in the Lushai campaign, 1871-72 (medal and clasp); civil surgn., Dinajpur, 1873, Jessor, 1875, Hugli, 1876-86; lecturer on botany, Hugli coll., 1877-86; offg. protector of emigrants, Calcutta, April, 1888; sanitary commr., Bengal, Jan., 1889; civil surgn., Burdwan, April, 1896; retd., Dec., 1899.

GREGORY, GREGORY MARCAR, late Opium Dept., Bengal.—Entered the dept., April, 1873; head asst., April, 1879; sub. dep. opium agent, March, 1890; factory supt., Benares, March, 1899; retd., May, 1904.

GREIG, GEORGE, late Forest Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived, 20th Sept., 1857, as a lieut., 93rd Sutherland highlanders; retd. from the army, June, 1865; joined the forest dept., Dec., 1868, and was apptd. dep. conservator, March, 1871, and conservator, Nov., 1876; retd., 1889.

GREY, LEOPOLD JOHN HERBERT, C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 3rd Jan., 1857; asst. commr. in Assam, Feb., 1865; offg. civil officer with Bhutan Dwar field force, Dec., 1865; from April, 1866, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., but was employed under the govt. of Bengal, 1868-69; pol. agent, Bahawalpur state, 1871-73, and 1877; jud. asst., July, 1876; on special duty in connection with imperial assemblage, 1876-77; dep. commr., Nov., 1876; sec. to board of examrs. (under govt. of India), March, 1882; commr. and supt., Delhi divn., March, 1886; offd. as first finl. commr. in 1891; and as second in 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Dec., 1894.

GREY, WILLIAM FRANCIS HUNGERFORD, Lieut.-Col., I.S.C., late Burma commn.—First commn., Aug., 1868; served in Burma from April, 1874, as asst. commr., and offg. asst. sec.; boundary officer, Paungde, Aug., 1881; cantonmt. mag., and small cause court judge, Thayetmyo, Sept., 1882; dep. commr., Aug., 1886; commr., Oct., 1898; retd., March, 1901.

GRIERSON, GEORGE ABRAHAM, C.I.E., Ph.D., D.Lit., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and small cause court judge; on famine duty, 1874; on special duty in the rev. dept., Dec., 1882; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; mag. and collr., March, 1890; opium agent, Bihar, Oct., 1896; on special duty with Linguistic Survey of India, May, 1898; retd., Sept., 1903; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; has publ. various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translns., collections of folk songs, &c.; also 'Curiosities of Indian Literature,' 1885; 'Vidyapati and his Contemporaries,' 1885; 'Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan,' 1890; and 'Inscriptions of Piyadasi,' 1891.

GRIERSON, JOHN, Col., Indian Army, late India Public Works Dept.—2nd lieut., Bombay artillery, Dec., 1857; arrived, 24th Jan., 1858; joined the dept., Feb., 1864; exec. engr., Jan., 1871; from May, 1872, served in various parts of India as dep. controller of accts., dep. examr., and examr. of accts.; reverted to mil. dept. Aug., 1894.

GRIESBACH, CARL LUDOLF, C.I.E., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India—Apptd. asst. supt., 26th Sept., 1878; arrived, 11th Nov., 1878; on special duty with troops in S. Afghanistan, 1880; present at the battle of Maiwand and during the siege of Kandahar (medal); employed on Afghan boundary commn., 1884-86; dep. supt., Nov., 1886; on depn. as geologist to the Amir of Kabul, 1888-89; supt., June, 1889; dir., July, 1894; retd., Feb., 1903;

author of numerous contributions to the survey publs.

GRIFFIN, HENRY DALY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at French Coll., Black-rock, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1889; joint mag., July, 1890; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1896; ditto, and second sec. to govt., Nov., 1902, to April, 1903.

GRIFFIN, SIR LEPEL HENRY, K.C.S.I., late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1870; supt., Kapurthala state, April, 1875 dep. commr., April, 1877; sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1878; on special duty at the Paris exhibn., July, 1878; during the occupation of Afghanistan held the special appt. of chief pol. officer in north and west districts of that country (medal); rest. and agent to the govr.-gen., Central India, Feb., 1881; K.C.S.I., May, 1881; royal commr., Indian and colonial exhibn., 1886; retd., Jan., 1889; author of 'Punjab Chiefs,' 1865, 'The Law of Inheritance to Chiefships,' 1869, 'The Great Republic,' 1884, and 'Famous Monuments of Central India,' 1886.

GRIFFITH, HUGH, Brigade-Surgn., late Med. Dept., Burma.—Joined the Indian med. dept. (Madras establ.), 1859; apptd. civil surgn. and health officer, Rangoon, April, 1871; also supt. of lunatic asylum, June, 1871, and supt. of vaccn., May, 1875; retd., May, 1890.

GRIFFITH, RALPH THOMAS HOTCHKIN, C.I.E. M.A., late Educn. Dept., N.W. Prov.—Arrived 1st Dec., 1853, and served as profir. and head master, Benares coll.; principal, Benares coll., Jan., 1861; supt. of observatory, Benares, May, 1866; inspr.-gen. of schs., N.W. Prov., March, 1878; dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1879; retd., 1885.

GRIGG, EDWARD EVANS, Lieut.-Col., Bengal Infantry, late Oudh Commn.—Arrived, as ensign, Dec., 1861; served under the mil. dept. till Jan., 1864, and subsequently as asst. commr. in Assam up to March, 1867, when he was transf. to the Oudh commn. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1855; offg. commr., 1892, and from April, 1894; commr., Kumaun, Nov., 1895; returned to Europe, March, 1899.

GRIMES, ARTHUR JOHN LUND, A.I.E.E., India Tel. Dept. (supt.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., 23rd Sept., 1885; supt., Dec., 1899; has received two medals with three clasps.

GRIMES, HENRY ROBERT, late Jail Dept., Madras.—Asst. dep. commr. of paper currency, Vizagapatam, 29th Nov., 1864; from April, 1866, served as supt. of the central jail, Coimbatore; inspr.-gen. of jails, Madras, July, 1884; retd., April, 1889.

GRIMLEY, REGINALD FEAR, Indian C.S. (head asst. colbr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 9th Nov., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Cent. Prov., April, 1898; asst. commr., May, 1898; dep. commr., Jan., 1900; spcl. asst. collr. and mag., Tinnevelly, July, 1900.

GRISH CHUNDER CHATTERJEE, B.L., late Bengal Judl. Dept.—oined the service as

munsif, 13th July, 1869; sub. judge, Oct., 1885; retd., Jan., 1903.

GRIST, FREDERIC EDWIN, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 23rd Jan., 1902.

GROVES, GUSTAVUS BARTON, late Postal Dept. Govt. of India.—Entered the dept. May, 1869; apptd. dep. postmr.-gen. for Rajputana Oct., 1894; offg. postmr.-gen., Bombay, Aug. 1901; ditto, Madras, April, 1902; confd. in that appt., April, 1903; retd., Nov., 1903; author of 'The Post Office in British Burma.'

GROVES, HERBERT, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 23rd July, 1868, and posted to Mysore; exec. engr., Feb., 1876; transf. to Central India, Oct., 1882, and employed on state railways; transf. to Burma, May, 1887 (medal, with clasp); services lent to Mysore state, July, 1890; under sec. to govt. of Mysore, railway branch, July, 1891; joint sec., July, 1892; transf. to Burma, April, 1895; suptg. engr., Nov., 1895; to Madras, Oct., 1899; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Burma, Aug., 1900; retd., Sept., 1903.

GROWSE, EDWARD FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Charter-house, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1879; arrived, 18th Nov., 1881, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1890; mag. and collr., July, 1894; mag. of Cuttack, Dec., 1895.

GRUNDY, JAMES (inspr. of mines).—Apptd. inspr. of mines in India, Nov., 1893.

GRUNING, JOHN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899.

GRUZELIER, OCTAVIUS, India Office (2nd class clerk, pol. dept.).—Educ. at Devonport High School; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, Registry and Record Dept., May, 1900; transf. to pol. dept., Jan., 1902.

GUBBAY, MOSES MORDECAI SIMEON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of customs; compiled the gen. admn. report, 1901-02.

GUISE, RIVETT FRANCIS, Bengal Police Dept. (dist. supt. of police).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1874, as offg. asst. supt. of police; confd., Nov., 1876; dist. supt., Sept., 1886.

GUITERMAN, CHARLES EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

GUNGARAM, C.I.E., Rai Bahadur, M.I.M.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 2nd Oct., 1873, and posted to Punjab provl. establ.; exec. engr., May, 1883; C.I.E., June, 1903; retd., Oct., 1903.

GUNION, ROBERT HUME, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (profir., Lahore Coll.).—Apptd. principal, Resdy. Coll., Indore, 18th Feb., 1890;

principal, Daly Coll., Indore, Oct., 1900; profr., Lahore College, Oct., 1903.

GUNN, WILLIAM DONALD, Major, Indian Civ. Vety. Dept. (supt., civ. vety. dept., Madras).—Joined the (home) service, 13th July, 1878; supt., civil veterinary dept., Bengal, May, 1892; supt., Hissar cattle farm, April, 1899; supt., civ. vety. dept., Madras, March, 1902.

GUPTA, B. L. (see Behari Lal Gupta).

GUPTA, JNANENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1900.

GUPTA, KALI PADAM, M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., Surgn.-Lieut.-Col., late Bengal Medical Dept.—Joined the service, 13th June, 1872, and served as supt. of vaccn., and on gen. and jail duty; in 1888 acted in addn. as sanitary commr. for Bengal; civil surgn., July, 1891; retd., June, 1908.

GUPTA, K. G. (see Krishna Govinda Gupta).

GUPTA, M. (see Mahananda Gupta).

GURDON, BERTRAND EVELYN MELLISH, C.I.E., D.S.O., Capt., Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent in Gilgit).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1886; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, April, 1895; ditto, Chitral, Oct., 1895; C.I.E., May, 1900; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, July, 1900; again asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1900, to Oct., 1902; pol. agent in Gilgit, Oct., 1903.

GURDON, EVELYN PULTENEY, Major Gen., Indian Army, late Punjab Commn.—Arrived, 2nd April, 1852; from Aug., 1860, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., dep. commr., and small cause court judge; addl. commr., Nov., 1881; commr. and supt., Aug., 1884; divl. judge, Oct., 1885; retd. from civil employment, Jan., 1890.

GURDON, PHILIP RICHARD THORNHAGH, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Assam).—Joined the army, 2nd Sept., 1882; apptd. asst. commr. in Assam, 2nd June, 1886; dep. commr. Feb., 1895.

GÜTERSLOH, FREDERIC NICHOLAS, late Railway Branch, Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., June, 1874, as asst. engr. on the Indus valley state railway; manager, Wardhavalley railway, Dec., 1878; locomotive supt., Rajputana railways, March, 1880; retd., June, 1891.

GWYTHER, FRANK EDWIN, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., and posted to Punjab irrigation, 31st May, 1881; asst. engr., Dec., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1895.

GWYTHER, HENRY THOMAS, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. as apprentice engr., 21st March, 1874, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., March, 1875; transfed. to State Railways, Aug., 1875; exec. engr., Oct., 1889.

GWYTHER, WILLIAM BANKS, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supt. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. to the dept., Nov., 1876, and posted to Bengal; asst. engr., April, 1878; exec. engr., March, 1892; supt. engr., Oct., 1903.

HABIB-ULLAH, SAIYID, Maulvi, Statutory C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. mag. and collr., N.W. Prov., under the statutory

rules, 14th Feb., 1887; joint mag., Sept., 1892; asst. commr., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., March, 1897; joint mag., Nov., 1898.

HADI, M. (see Muhammad Hadi).

HAFFKINE, W. M., C.I.E. (director-in-chief, Plague Research Laby., Bombay).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1896, and was placed on duty in the Plague Research Laby.; C.I.E., June, 1897; dir.-in-chief, Dec., 1901.

HAFIZ MUHAMMAD NAWAZ KHAN, BAHADUR, Lieut.-Colonel.—Apptd. British Agent with the Ameer of Afghanistan, April, 1900.

HAIG, CHARLES THOMAS, Major-Gen., R.E., late Survey of India Dept.—Obtained his first commn. from Addiscombe, June, 1854; lieut., Bombay engrs., May, 1856; served with the Persian expdn., 1856-57 (medal and clasp), and with the Rajputana field force, 1857 (medal and clasp); asst. exec. engr. at Nimach, May, 1858; apptd. as asst. in the great trigon. survey of India, Sept., 1859; served with the Okhamandal field force in 1859; dep. supt., Oct., 1867; on depn. as commr., Venice geographical exhibn., 1881; dep. surveyor-gen. in charge of trigon. survey branch, Feb., 1887; retd. from civil employment, 1890; and from the army, April, 1891.

HAIGH, PERCY BARNES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Leys Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

HAILEY, HAMMETT REGINALD CLODE, B.A., Indian C.S. (settl. offr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 7th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1897; joint mag., June, 1899; settl. offr., Dec., 1899.

HAILEY, WILLIAM MALCOLM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1898, to Oct., 1899; colonization offr., Jhelum Canal, Nov., 1901.

HAINES, SIR FREDERICK PAUL, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., C.I.E., Field-Marshal.—Entered the army as ensign in 1839; mil. sec. to Sir Hugh Gough in the war on the Sutlej, 1845; was present at the battles of Mudki and Firozshah (medal and clasp); served through the Punjab campaign of 1848 and 1849, and was present at Ramnagar, the passage of the Chenab, and the battles of Chilianwala and Gujrat; served with the 21st fusiliers through the campaign of the Crimea, 1854-55 (medal with four clasps, 5th class; of the Medjidie and Turkish medal); K.C.B., 1871; G.C.B., 1877; commander-in-chief of the Madras army, and member of the govr.'s council, May, 1871, to 1875; commander-in-chief of the forces in India, with local rank of gen. and extraordinary member of the govr.-gen.'s council, April, 1876, to 1881, and directed the mil. operations of the Afghan campaigns of 1878-79 and 1879-80 (G.C.S.I.; thanked by both houses of parliament, 1879 and 1881).

HAINES, HERMANN ANDERSON, India Office (asst. sec., public works dept.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford; junr. clerk, finl. dept., 15th Nov., 1881; pol. dept., 1886;

précis writer, Dec., 1889, to Dec., 1894; senr. clerk, Feb., 1894; reading clerk to the Council of India, Aug., 1897; asst. sec., public works dept., Oct., 1903.

HAKIM, H. M. (see Hormasji Merwanji Hakim).

HAKIM, M. H. (see Muhammad Hussein Hakim).

HALDAR, M. L. (see Mati Lal Haldar).

HALL, ARTHUR ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., Teachers' Coll., Saidapet).—Apptd. principal, Teachers' Coll., Madras, Jan., 1887.

HALL, GEOFFREY CRAYTHORNE, Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 30th March, 1872, as asst. surgn., Bengal med. establ.; served under the mil. dept. until March, 1875, when he entered civil employ as offg. civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Jhansi; supt., central prison, Allahabad, Nov., 1877; inspr.-gen. of prisons, July, 1896; administrative med. offr. and sany. commr., Central Prov., Mar., 1902; retd., Nov., 1902; author of three med. works.

HALL, HAROLD FIELDING PATRICK, Burma Commn. (asst. commr.).—Joined the service, 30th July, 1886; extra asst. commr., Feb. 1888; asst. commr., June, 1890; small cause court judge, Jan. to June, 1892.

HALL, MONTAGU STANLEY, India Office (staff clerk, registry and record dept.).—Writer, India office, 21st Jan., 1875; clerk, 2nd class, Oct., 1878; staff clerk, Jan., 1883.

HALL, WILLIAM THOMAS, C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Rossall, Kingstown Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1876; arrived, 5th Dec., 1878, and served in British Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dir. of land records and agric., Oct., 1887; on special duty to Perak tin mines in 1888-89; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; acting prest., Rangoon municipality, March, 1894; offg. commr. and sessions judge, April, 1896; commr., Mar., 1899; member of legisl. council, Burma, June, 1902; finl. commr., Dec., 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1904.

HALLETT, HENRY HONYWOOD HUGHES, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., Central Prov.—First commn., July, 1858; arrived, 11th Aug., 1858; served with the Bundelkhand field force, 1859; from 26th Dec., 1868, served in the Central Prov. as personal asst. to chief commr., asst. commr., &c., and was apptd. supt. of the sch. of industry and central jail, Jabalpur, Aug., 1878; also supt. of reformatory sch., June, 1891; inspr.-gen. of police and jails, Oct., 1894, to Aug., 1897.

HALLETT, JOHN REVANS, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at the Univ. of Melbourne; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 17th Oct., 1864; asst. mag. and collr., Bardwan, May, 1865; inspr. of schs., Aug., 1870; second inspr. of registrn. offices, March, 1876; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; district and sessions judge, Sept., 1880; retd., Feb., 1890.

HALLIDAY, FREDERICK LOCH, Bengal Police Dept. (dep. commr. of police, Calcutta).—Offd. as asst. supt. of police from 3rd Sept., 1885; confd., Nov., 1888; dist. supt., June, 1895; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1902.

HALLIDAY, FREDERICK MYTTON, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 30th Oct., 1856, and served in Bengal as asst. mag.

and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; offd. as opium agent, Benares, in 1876 and 1877; commr., April, 1878; chairman, port commrs., Calcutta, April, 1887; offd. at various periods as addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council; member of board of rev., April, 1889; retd., Nov., 1891.

HALLIDAY, HUGH MACLEAN, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Assam).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; 1st commn., 30th Jan., 1886; joined the Assam commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1901.

HALLIFAX, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 28th Dec., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr., Nov., 1892; joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal, May, 1898; mag. and collr., June, 1900.

HALLIFAX, CHARLES JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and All Souls' Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 31st Dec., 1888, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; councillor to the Raja of Suket, 1891-93; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Dec., 1896; dep. commr., April, 1898; sec. to plague commr., Oct., 1898; judl. and genl. sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1901, to Nov., 1903.

HALLIFAX, HENRY FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Sept., 1893, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.

HALLWARD, NORMAN LESLIE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (profr., presy. Coll., Calcutta).—Apptd. profr., Dacca Coll., 9th Feb., 1890; profr., presy. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1897, and Dec., 1901.

HAM, WILLIAM JAMES, late Postal Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the service, and was attached to the sea post office, Bombay, 6th May, 1868; supernumerary inspr., Oct., 1877; dep. postmr.-gen., Central India, May, 1880, and at Calcutta, attached to the railway mail service, April, 1882; asst. dir.-gen., June, 1887; inspr.-gen., railway mail service, Sept., 1890; has acted as postmr.-gen. at Lucknow, Calcutta, Madras, N.W. Prov. and Oudh; confd. as postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1897; retd., April, 1901.

HAMBLIN, RICHARD EBB, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Christ Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 4th Jan., 1878, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., June, 1887; joint mag., March, 1890; dep. commr., April, 1893; mag. and collr., Sept., 1894; commr., March, 1899.

HAMILTON, FRANK SUMNER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 10th Dec., 1885, and served as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1892; mag. and collr., Oct., 1896; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1897.

HAMILTON, RIGHT HON. LORD GEORGE FRANCIS, G.C.S.I., M.P.—Born Dec., 1845; educ. at Harrow; ensign, rifle brigade, 1864; transf. to the Coldstream guards, 1868; was elected member for Middlesex, Dec., 1868, and represented that county in parliament until the election which followed the reform bill of 1885, when he was returned for the Ealing divn.; under sec. of state for India, Feb., 1874; vice-prest. of the committee of council on educn., April, 1878; first lord of the admiralty in the Marquis of Salisbury's first and second administrations, 1885-86, and 1886-92; chairman, London school board, 1894-95; sec. of state for India in Lord Salisbury's third administration, July, 1895, and fourth administration, Nov., 1900; G.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; resigned, Oct., 1903.

HAMILTON, JOHN MONTGOMERY, India Office (second class clerk, store dept.).—Educ. at Ayr Acad.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 18th Nov., 1901.

HAMILTON, RICHARD ERRIS, late Finl. Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the dept., 1st Aug., 1868; served as asst. acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov., and British Burma, asst. compr.-gen., and asst. commr. of paper currency; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1882; compr. and dep. commr. of paper currency, Central Prov., Jan., 1883; compr., India treasuries, Nov., 1891; acctnt.-gen., Punjab, April, 1894; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1896; acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1898; ret'd., Sept., 1904.

HAMILTON, ROBERT CLIFTON, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 1st Dec., 1893, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. officer; asst. mag. and collr., July, 1899; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1900; asst. collr. on famine duty, Belgaum, April, 1901; author of a grammar of Daffa language.

HAMILTON, THOMAS SHARP, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Old Trafford Sch., Manchester, Chester Coll., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 6th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. judge, Nov., 1887; judge and sessions judge, April, 1890; ret'd., Jan., 1897.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM ROBARTS, Barr. (1890), late Police Dept., Bombay. —Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1869; asst. to the rev. and police commr., Oct., 1869; dep. collr., May, 1874; city mag., Sept., 1874; Huzur dep. commr., July, 1876; presidency mag., Jan., 1888 to 1899; ret'd. 16th Aug., 1899; author of a police manual, and works on local self-govt. and village customs.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM STIRLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Winchester, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, April, 1897; senr. sec., April, 1898, to Nov., 1900; dep. commr., March, 1904.

HAMMICK, MURRAY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (sec. to govt., rev. dept., Madras).—Educ. at the Charterhouse; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 18th Dec., 1877, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in Coorg as asst.

commr. and mag.; from Jan., 1883, served in the Madras secretariat as asst. sec., jud. and legis. depts., and acting under sec. in the rev. dept.; head asst., S. Kanara, April, 1885; acting postmr.-gen., Madras, 1886; sec. to the commrs. of land rev., Oct., 1887; collr. and mag., Malabar, Nov., 1893; inspr.-gen. of Madras police, Dec., 1894; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; sec. to govt., local, muncl., educ. and legis. depts., Dec., 1901; addl. member of legis. Council, Madras, Nov., 1901, May, 1902, and March, 1903; acting chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1903; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1903.

HAMMICK, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 21st Nov., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and tutor to the Maharaja of Kolhapur; on famine duty, Kaladgi, Sept., 1877; jud. asst. to the pol. agent, Kathiawar, Aug., 1883; judge and sessions judge, Shikarpur, Feb., 1885; member of Deccan agriculturists' relief act commn., 1891-92; ret'd., June, 1894.

HAMMOND, ALFRED JOHN, late Punjab Police Dept.—Entered the service as asst. dist. supt. of police, July, 1870; dist. supt., July, 1880; dep. inspr. genl., July, 1901; ret'd., May, 1903.

HAMMOND, EGBERT LAURIE LUCAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (supt., Cooch Behar State).—Educ. at Newton Coll., S. Devon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 27th Nov., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt., Cooch Behar State, Nov., 1903.

HAMNETT, FREDERICK HARPER, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras).—Educ. at Clifton Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 17th Nov., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, March, 1885; acting sub-collr., Godavari, April, 1886; head asst., Nov., 1887; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1891; district and sessions judge, Feb., 1895; coll. and mag., Oct., 1899; dist. and sess. judge, Godavari, April, 1901.

HANCE, JOHN EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Cent. Prov.).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 4th Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Prov. as asst. commr.

HANCOCK, GEORGE EDWARD, Col., Indian Army, late Pol. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 13th Nov., 1859, and served under the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1863, served as asst. supt. of rev. survey and assessment, Gujarat; acting supt. of the Rajkot and Limbi states, and acting asst. rest. at Aden, and at Baroda; asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti, Oct., 1871; asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, July, 1872; from Oct., 1873, served as supt. of surveys under rajasthanik court, Kathiawar; acting joint administrator of Gondal state, 1880-81, and was on special duty with the Thakur Saheb in Europe, 1883; on special duty to inquire into administration of Rajpipla state, Nov., 1883; third asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1885, continuing to be supt. of surveys; prest. of the rajasthanik court, Jan., 1886; pol. supt., Savantvadi, May-Oct., 1891; pol. agent, &c., Kolhapur; offd. in Kathiawar, Jan., 1894; and again from March, 1895; returned to Europe, May, 1896.

HANDCOCK, GEORGE FRASER, Madras Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1881; exec. engr., Feb., 1894.

HANDCOCK, WILLIAM ERSKINE FRASER, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., May, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

HANDLEY, ALFRED PERCIVAL, Barr. (1872), late Jud.
