THOMSON, JOHN BROWN, M.A., Indian C.S. (member, board of revenue, United Prov.).—Educ. at the Acad., and Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, Nov., 1870, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; dep. supt. of family domains of Maharaja of Benares, Nov., 1882; registrar of the high court, May, 1884; mag. and collr., April, 1889; offd. as sec. to govt. in 1889 and 1890; inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1894; commr., July, 1900; member, board of revenue, Nov., 1902.

THOMSON, ROBERT GEORGE, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Islington Proprietary Sch., and Univ. of London; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 13th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Dec., 1882; senr. sec., March, 1886; postmr.-gen., Punjab, Feb., 1888; junr. sec. to govt. of Punjab, April, 1890; rev. sec., April, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; ret'd., Aug., 1899.

THOMSON, SAMUEL JOHN, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (sany. commr, United Prov.).—Joined the service as surgn., 1st Oct., 1877; arrived, 27th April, 1878, and went through Afghan campaign, 1880 (medal); dep. sanitary commr., May, 1885; sanitary commr. and supt. of vaccination, July, 1896; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; on deputation to S. Africa, Feb., 1902, to Jan., 1903; author of 'Sanitary Principles.'

THOMSON, WILLIAM BOYD, Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Belfast Methodist Coll., Trinity Coll., Dublin, and the Royal Univ. of Ireland; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 16th Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.,

and asst. settlt. officer; asst. mag. and collr., Feb., 1897; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1898.

THORBURN, SEPTIMUS SMET, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; jud. asst., 1st grade, Jan., 1881; dep. commr., April, 1883; commr. and supt., Jan., 1892; finl. commr., April, 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1899; author of 'Bannu; or, Our Afghan Frontier,' 'David Leslie,' 'Asiatic Neighbours,' 'Muslimans and Money-lenders,' 'Report on Peasant Indebtedness and Land Alienations to Money-lenders,' a report on the settlt. of the Bannu district, &c., &c.

THORBURN, WALTER MILLER, B.A., Barr. (1898), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dalhousie Coll. and Univ., Halifax, Univ. of Edinburgh, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 26th Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1884; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1890; retired, Dec., 1902.

THORNHILL, GEORGE, C.S.I., late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Rugby and Haileybury, and apptd. in 1842; arrived, 7th April, 1843; served in Madras, as asst. to collr. and mag. in various districts, and was employed as civil officer in connection with the construction of the Godavari and Kistna anicuts; principal asst., Ganjam, 1856; collr. and mag., Kistna, 1861; member of board of rev., 1870-78; was also apptd. dir. of bank of Madras, and commr. for uncov. civil service exams., 1875; ret'd., 1878.

THORNHILL, HENRY BEAUFOY, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (inspg. offr. of cantonmts.).—First commn., 23rd April, 1873; arrived, 1st Nov., 1873; on field service, Afghanistan, 1878-79 (medal); cantonmt. mag., Feb., 1887; dep. supt., Port Blair, May, 1888; cantonmt. mag., N.W. Prov., June, 1891; C.I.E., June, 1903; inspg. offr. of cantonmts., June, 1903.

THORNHILL, JOSEPH HELEN, M.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd., 15th July, 1869, and employed on irrign. works in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Sept., 1879; ret'd. March, 1897.

THORNTON, ARTHUR PARRY, C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—Ensign, 14th foot, 14th Sept., 1867; admitted to the Bengal staff corps, Sept., 1871; on famine duty, 1874; served under the foreign dept. from Jan., 1875, and acted as asst. agent, Rajputana, and as mag., Mount Abu, and asst. gen. supt. for thagi and dakaiti; attaché to foreign office during Delhi durbar, 1876-77; on mil. duty, 1878-79; from March, 1879, served as cantonmt. mag. at Morar, and at Nasirabad; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Jan., 1882; cantonmt. mag. and small cause court judge, Secunderabad, Feb., 1884; pol. agent, Bhopawar, May, 1886; Haraoti and Tonk, March, 1889; offg. commr., Ajmir, March, 1895; rest. and commr., Ajmir, Feb., 1897; rest., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1900; offg. agent to gov.-genl. in Rajputana, April, 1901, to Feb., 1902; C.S.I., June, 1902; placed on U.S. list, Feb., 1903.

THORNTON, EDWARD LABOUCHERE RUTHVEN, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Marlborough, and King's Coll., Cambridge;

apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 5th Dec., 1891, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; acted as manager, Jaipur estate, 1893; head asst., Dec., 1898.

THORNTON, HUGH AYLMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

THORNTON, JAMES HOWARD, C.B., B.A., M.D., Dep.-Surgn.-Gen., late Bengal Med. Service.—First commn., 1856; from 9th Nov., 1861, served as civil asst. surgn. and civil surgn., besides service under the mil. dept.; principal med. officer with the Indian forces, Egypt, 1885; C.B., Aug., 1885; dep. surgn.-gen., Punjab frontier force, June, 1886; ret'd., Aug., 1891; author of 'Memories of Seven Campaigns,' 1895.

THORNTON, LESLIE MELVILLE, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1878; arrived, 1st Jan., 1881, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sec. to govt., Oct., 1883; under sec., Oct., 1886; offd. as finl. sec., 1891; joint mag., Aug., 1891; offg. legal remembrancer, and member of, and sec. to, provl. legisl. council, Dec., 1892, to March, 1893; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1894; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1898; second sec. to govt., March, 1899; district and sessions judge, Nov., 1902.

THORNTON, THOMAS HENRY, C.S.I., D.C.L., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1855; asst. commr., Punjab, 1857-59; personal asst. to finl. commr., Punjab, 1859-60; personal asst. to jud. commr., 1860-62; small cause court judge, Lahore, 1862-63; dep. commr., Delhi, 1863-64; sec. to the govt. of the Punjab, 1864-78, and was on two occasions temporarily in charge of the administration of the province; acted as foreign sec. to the govt. of India, 1876-77; member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 1877-79; judge, chief court, Punjab, 1878-81; ret'd., 1881; fellow of univ. of Calcutta, 1875; vice-prest., univ. coll., Lahore, 1879; author of a life of Sir Robert Sandeman.

THUILLIER, SIR HENRY EDWARD LANDOR, Kt., C.S.I., F.R.S., Gen., R.A., late Survey of India.—Educ. at Addiscombe, 1831-32; first commn., Bengal artillery, Dec., 1832; served in the survey dept. from Dec., 1836, and conducted the surveys of Jaintia, the Puri divn. of Cuttack, and the district of Cachar, and part of Sylhet; dep. surveyor-gen. of India, Jan., 1847, to March, 1861, when he was apptd. surveyor-gen.; ret'd., Dec., 1877; C.S.I., May, 1870; knighted in 1879; col. commandant, R.A., Jan., 1883; joint author of the 'Manual of Survey for India.'

THUILLIER, SIR HENRY RAVENSHAW, K.C.I.E., Col., R.E., late Survey of India.—Entered Addiscombe, Aug., 1855; lieut., Bengal engrs., June, 1857; apptd. to the survey dept. as asst. in the great trigonl. survey, May, 1859; dep. supt., Sept., 1869; offg. supt., great trigonl. survey, April-Oct., 1876; dep. surveyor-gen. in charge of the rev. survey branch, Oct., 1884; surveyor-gen., Feb., 1887; C.I.E., June, 1893; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1895; ret'd., March, 1895.

THURBURN, JAMES WHITE, C.S.I., Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 7th May, 1872, and employed in the mil. works branch in the Punjab; served in the Afghan war, 1878-79 (medal); reverted to home establ., Nov., 1880; re-apptd., Jan., 1884, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1884; on field service on the Punjab frontier, Sept., 1897-Jan., 1898 (medal and three clasps); suptg. engr., July, 1899; chief engr. and sec. to govt., Punjab, Aug., 1902; C.S.I., June, 1903; transf. to unemployed list, Dec., 1903.

THURSTON, JOHN NEVILLE OLDFIELD (dep. commr., Burma).—Joined the service, March, 1886, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1900.

TICKELL, CHARLES, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1875, and employed on irrign. works in the Punjab; exec. engr., Nov., 1884; under sec. to govt., public works dept., 1890-92; services lent to Kashmir state, 1892-94; ret'd., March, 1897.

TICKELL, JAMES ROBERT, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to state railways; lent to B.B. and C.I. Rly. Co. from Jan., 1885; exec. engr., Aug., 1893.

TICKELL, RICHARD HUGH, India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to Central India; transf. to Punjab, June, 1890; exec. engr., May, 1894.

TIDY, WILLIAM MEYMOTT, M.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 28th Nov., 1860; served in the N.W. Prov. from Aug., 1862, as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Sept., 1878; ret'd., 1886.

TIGHE, MICHAEL AUGUSTUS, Major, Indian Army, Foreign Dept., Govt. of India (pol. agent, Sibi).—First commn., April, 1881; apptd. to pol. dept., August, 1887; pol. agent, southern Baluchistan, April, 1896; dep. commr. and pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, April, 1898; retransf. to S. Baluchistan, July, 1899; pol. agent, Malwa, Oct., 1902; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, 1903.

TILLY, HARRY LINDSAY, A.M.I.C.E. (dep. commr., and chief collr. of customs, Burma).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the public works dept., 1st Oct., 1877, and served in Burma as asst. engr., personal asst. to chief engr., and exec. engr.; transf. to the commn. as asst. commr., Dec., 1885; sec. to finl. commr., 1893-94, and 1894-95; dep. commr., May, 1898; and chief collr. of customs, April, 1900; K-i-H. medal, June, 1904.

TINDALL, CHRISTIAN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 27th Nov. 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

TINNEY, GEORGE FREDERICK, India Office (second class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 4th July, 1882.

TIPNIS, R. S. (see Raghunath Shivram Tipnis).

TODD - NAYLOR, HENRY PAUL, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 14th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Burma as asst. commr., March, 1886; dep. commr., May, 1890; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; commr., April, 1902.

TODHUNTER, CHARLES GEORGE, Indian C.S. (sub-sollr., and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at Aldenham Gr. Sch. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. registrar, high court, July, 1895, acted as registrar, appellate side, from May, 1896; head asst. collr., June, 1897; dep. commr., salt, etc., rev., Nov., 1897; sec. to commr., salt, &c., rev., Oct., 1900; sub-collr. and joint mag., June, 1903.

TOLLINTON, HENRY PHILLIPS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Leamington Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 17th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Jan., 1899, to Nov., 1900; dep. commr. April, 1904.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM DOUGLAS, India Office (2nd class clerk, registry and record dept.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 24th Jan., 1902.

TOMKINS, WILLIAM PERCIVAL, C.I.E., Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., June, 1860; joined the public works dept., June, 1863, as asst. engr., Assam; transf. to British Burma, Jan., 1865; acted as asst. to chief engr. and asst. sec. to chief commr., 1865; exec. engr., Oct., 1866; transf. to mil. works branch, Nov., 1873; asst. sec. to govt. of India, civil and mil. works branch, April, 1880; suptg. engr. and sec. to agent, Baluchistan, 1882; suptg. engr., Aug., 1884; took part in Zohar valley expedn., Sept., 1884; C.I.E., Jan., 1890; chief engr., Sept., 1890; dir.-gen. of mil. works, April, 1893; returned to Europe, June, 1896.

TOOGOOD, JOHN HENRY, India Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at Calcutta Civil Engrg. Coll.; apptd., 11th Oct., 1871, and employed on irrig. works in Bengal; on famine relief duty in Madras, 1877-78; exec. engr., Nov., 1881; offg. under sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., April-Nov., 1894; supt. of famine relief works, 2nd circle, Feb., 1897; suptg. engr., March, 1898.

TOTTENHAM, LOFTUS RICHARD, late Bengal C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury; arrived, 11th July, 1856, and served in Bengal as asst., joint mag. and dep. collr., and mag. and collr.; also as supt. of stamps and stationery, Jan., 1866, and registrar of the high court, Dec., 1866; from Jan., 1874, served in the jud. dept. as district and sessions judge, and offd. as supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Feb., 1878; judge of the high court, Calcutta, July, 1879; retd., March, 1893.

TOWERS, GRAINGER LAWRENCE, M.I.E.E., late India Telegraph Dept.—Appointed asst. supt., Nov., 1870; in charge of field telegraphs, Perak expedn. (medal and clasp) 1875-6; attached to Kabul field telegraphs, 1880 (Afghanistan medal); supt., Oct., 1887; chief supt., Feb., 1898; retd., Feb., 1901.

TOWERS, ROBERT MASON, M.A., late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 21st Jan., 1863, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from July, 1867, served in the jud. dept. as small cause court judge and district and sessions judge; from Jan., 1878, to Feb., 1880, served as offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur; retd., 1888.

TOWNSEND, CREWE ARMAND HAMILTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

TOWNSEND, EDWARD COPLESTON, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., 25th Aug., 1883; joined the Burma commn. as asst. commr., 12th Feb. 1887; dep. commr., March, 1901.

TOYNBEE, GEORGE, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 27th Nov., 1865, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt. of canal rev., Oct., 1871; on famine relief duty, Nov., 1873; offg. sec., board of rev., lower prov., Dec., 1874; second inspr. of registrn. offices, April, 1877; joint mag., Dec., 1877; mag. and collr., Feb., 1884; commr., May, 1891; member of board of rev., Calcutta, April, 1898; member of legisl. council, Nov., 1898; author of 'Sketch of the History of Orissa from 1803 to 1828,' 'Sketch of the Administration of the Hugli District from 1795 to 1845,' and 'A Chowkidari Manual'; retd., Nov., 1900.

TOZER, HENRY JOHN, M.A., India Office (junr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 19th Jan., 1882; B.A. (honours), London univ., 1889; M.A. (phil. and pol. econ.), 1893; transf. to rev. and stat. dept., 17th Dec., 1897; Society of Arts silver medal for paper on Indian trade, 1901; joint editor, India Office List, May to Oct., 1901; staff clerk, Oct., 1901; junr. clerk, Jan., 1904; author of 'British India and its Trade,' 1902.

TRACY, THOMAS BURROWES, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1857; arrived, 29th Dec., 1857, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Muttra, April, 1877; from Feb., 1883, served as civil and sessions judge, and acted as jud. commr. in Oudh, 1884-85; retd., Sept., 1887.

TRAFFORD, RICHARD WYLME, late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 28th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; district judge, Nov., 1884; divl. judge, Dec., 1886; retd., Oct., 1891.

TREASURE, WILLIAM HOUTON, India Office (asst. to the legal adviser).—Clerk to the legal adviser to the sec. of state, June, 1877; solicitor of the supreme court, 1887; asst. to the solicitor to the sec. of state, Aug., 1892.

TREMENHEERE, JAMES HENRY APPERLEY, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Lancing and Cheltenham Colls.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1875, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; in 1883 served in Mysore as acting civil and sessions

judge; served subsequently in Madras as head asst. collr., and acting asst. sec. to govt.; offg. private sec. to the govr., May to Aug., 1885; sub-collr. and joint mag., Aug., 1888; collr. and mag., Nilgiris, July, 1890; district and sessions judge, Aug., 1890; collr. and mag., Kistna, Jan., 1894; addl. sess. judge, Nilgiris, May, 1897; acted as sec. to govt. local, &c. depts., Nov., 1897, and June, 1898; confd., Sept., 1898; ret'd., Dec., 1901.

TREMLETT, JAMES DYER, M.A., Barr. (1872), late Bengal C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 17th Nov., 1860, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and dep. commr.; commr., Nov., 1880; addl. judge, chief court, April, 1884; divl. judge, Nov., 1884; judge, chief court, Punjab, Sept., 1886; ret'd., March, 1889.

TRETHEWY, ANTHONY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Winchester, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag., and asst. commr.; dep. commr., May, 1896; mag. and collr., April, 1897.

TREVELYAN, ERNEST JOHN, Barr. (1873), late Calcutta High Court.—Advocate, high court, Calcutta, 1875-85; offg. sec., board of examrs. for pleaders, &c., 1875-82; offg. mag., Calcutta, N. divn., 1876; judge, high court, Calcutta, 12th Jan., 1885; also prest., board of examrs. for pleaders and mukhtars, 1886; ret'd., May, 1898.

TREVOR, ALFRED SEPTIMUS, late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1878, and posted to state railways; exec. engr., Aug., 1888; ret'd., 8th April, 1901.

TREVOR, SIR ARTHUR CHARLES, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 28th Oct., 1861, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and dep. commr. of customs and opium; collr. of customs, Bombay, April, 1879; senr. collr., Dec., 1884; collr. of salt rev., Oct., 1885; commr. of customs, salt, opium, and abkari, and reporter-gen. of external commerce, Sept., 1888; also British delegate for the Portuguese treaty; commr. in Sind, Nov., 1890; member of Bombay council, Nov., 1892; C.S.I., May, 1894; temporary member of govr.-gen.'s council, April-Oct., 1895; confd., April, 1896; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1898; ret'd., May, 1901.

TREVOR, EDWARD WALTER, Col., Indian Army, late Sind Commn.—First commn., April, 1855; arrived, 8th May, 1855; from Nov., 1864, served in Sind as dep. collr. and mag., and acting district supt. of police; on mil. duty, 1879-81; dep. commr., Upper Sind frontier, Jan., 1881; collr. and mag., Hyderabad, Dec., 1884; at various periods acted as commr. in Sind; reverted to mil. dept., April, 1893.

TREVOR, FREDERICK GEORGE BRUNTON, C.I.E., late India Office.—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Marlborough Coll.; E. India house writer, old establt., 10th March, 1858; junr. clerk, public, eduel., and ecclesi. depts., Aug., 1860; senr. clerk, July, 1875; jud. and public dept., 1877; asst. sec. in that dept., June, 1887; dir. of funds, Feb., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

TREVOR, GEORGE HERBERT, C.S.I., Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt of India.—Joined the Madras artillery, 5th April, 1858; from June, 1862, served in the Central Prov. as inspr. of schs., cantonmt. mag., asst. sec. to chief commr., and asst. commr.; from Oct., 1867, acted as asst. rest., Hyderabad; sec. for Berar, April, 1877; depd. to accompany Sir Salar Jung on his tour to Europe in 1867 as pol. officer; offd. as rest. at Hyderabad, May-June, 1882; rest. and commr., Ajmir-Merwara, Nov., 1887; agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr. of Ajmir-Merwara, March, 1890; reverted to mil. dept., March, 1895; author of 'Rhymes of Rajputana.'

TREVOR, SALUSBURY THOMAS, Col., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—First commn., Dec., 1852; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1854; from Jan., 1855, served in British Burma as asst. engr., exec. engr., asst. to chief engr., and asst. sec. to chief commr.; transfed. to Bengal, Oct., 1863; served as garrison engr., Fort William, and suptg. engr., and was apptd. sec. to chief commr., Assam, public works dept., July, 1869; chief engr., and joint sec., public works dept., Bengal, March, 1881; on depn. in connection with international exhibn. buildings at Calcutta, July, 1883; chief engr. and sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., railways and roads and buildings, Jan., 1884; ret'd., March, 1889.

TREVOR, WILLIAM SPOTTISWOODE, V.C., Major-Gen., R.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Second lieut., Bengal engrs., Dec., 1849; arrived, 5th Feb., 1852; served in the Burmese war, 1852-53 (medal with clasps); employed on the Pegu survey, 1854; exec. engr., Bassein, Nov., 1855; was subsequently posted to Darjiling and Dinajpur; garrison engr., Fort William, 1861; compt. and examr. of accts., Bengal, May, 1863; served in the Bhutan campaign, 1865 (medal and V.C.); suptg. engr., Calcutta, Jan., 1867; consulting engr. to govt. of Bengal, irrign., Jan., 1868; suptg. engr., mil. works, Calcutta, 1872; addl. chief engr. for famine relief works, Bengal, 1874; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Indore, April-Dec., 1875; offg. chief engr. and sec. to chief commr., Burma, Feb., 1876; confd., Dec., 1878; dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec. to govt. of India, March, 1881; sec. to govt. of India in the public works dept., Feb., 1882; ret'd., Feb., 1887.

TRITTON, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

TROWARD, THOMAS, late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 9th Oct., 1869, and served in the Punjab as asst. cantonmt. mag., asst. commr., and offg. jud. asst.; dep. commr., Jan., 1887; divl. judge, May, 1889; ret'd., March, 1896.

TRUEMAN, THOMAS, Col., I.S.C., late Bombay Jud. Dept.—Arrived, 23rd Nov., 1856, and served in the Mutiny campaign under Sir Hugh Rose (medal and clasp), and subsequently as asst. cantonmt. mag. at Poona and Kirki, and cantonmt. mag., and city mag., Karachi; cantonmt. mag., Ahmadabad, April, 1877, and

subsequently at Belgaum; on famine duty, Sholapur, Sept., 1877; ret'd., Nov., 1888.

TUCKER, ALEXANDER LAUZUN PENDOCK, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (off. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan).—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 28th Dec., 1882, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; forest settlt. officer, Bijapur, Feb., 1836; asst. to rest. at Hyderabad, and asst. sec. for Berar, April, 1888; on depn. to Khetri state, 1888-89; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, April, 1890; in 1891 acted as under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept. 1st asst. to rest., Hyderabad, and sec. for Berar, Oct., 1893; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Dec., 1895; offg. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Aug., 1899; confd., Feb., 1902; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; on special duty in for. dept. secretariat, Jan., 1904; offg. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1905.

TUCKER, HENRY ST. GEORGE, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1864; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1865, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; jud. asst., Dec., 1883; dep. commr., Nov., 1884; commr. in Burma, May, 1886; ret'd., Sept., 1890; author of reports on the settlt. of the Dera Ismail Khan and Kohat districts.

TUCKER, HENRY PENDOCK ST. GEORGE, late Bombay C.S.—Apptd. from Haileybury in 1842; arrived, 14th April, 1843; served as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge in various districts in the Bombay presidency, and acted as dep. registrar of the sadar court in Bombay in 1845-46; acting first asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, July, 1858; acting civil auditor, Bombay, 1858-59; collr. and mag., N. Kanara, July, 1862; judge and sessions judge, Konkan, Aug., 1862; offg. judge of the high court, Bombay, June, 1863; member of council, Bombay, April, 1869; ret'd. in 1876.

TUCKER, LOUIS HENRY EMILE, C.I.E., Maj.-Gen.; late Police Dept., Punjab.—First commn., 1860; arrived, 11th April, 1861; from Sept., 1864, served in the police dept., Punjab, as asst. supt. and district supt.; on special duty in connection with Kabul war, Nov. 1878, and again, Sept., 1879; dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1888; chief commr., Port Blair, Sept., 1888; re-apptd. dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Punjab, May, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; inspr.-gen. and under sec. to govt., home (police) dept., Dec., 1894; ret'd., April, 1899.

TUCKER, WILLIAM ROXBURGH.—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1871, as acting asst. commr., Oudh; confd., June, 1873; dep. commr., July, 1894; ret'd., Dec., 1903.

TUDBALL, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Prov.).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 12th Nov., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1892; asst. commr., Feb., 1895; dep. commr., April, 1897; mag. and collr., March, 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1901.

TUDOR-OWEN, WILLIAM COURTENAY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

TUPP, ALFRED COTTERELL, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1861; arrived, 29th Nov., 1862, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. supt. of Dehra Dun; also subord. judge from March, 1873; held charge of prov. gazetteer, 1876-77; joint mag. and dep. collr., Azimgarh, Aug., 1877; transfld. to the finl. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1878, and served in Madras, Bombay, and Bengal; acctnt.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1883; Bombay, Dec., 1887; N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Nov., 1888; ret'd., Oct., 1889; author of 'The Indian Civil Service and the Competition System,' 1875; 'The Statistics of the N.W. Prov.,' 'The Paris International Monetary Conference of 1881, and Bimetallism,' &c., &c.

TUPPER, SIR CHARLES LEWIS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1869; arrived, 1st Nov., 1871, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; under sec. to govt. of Punjab, April, 1877; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, Sept., 1878; junr. sec. to govt., Punjab, March, 1882; sec., Nov., 1888; chief sec. to govt., March, 1890; on special duty in foreign dept., govt. of India, 1893-94; and from April, 1895; commr. and supt., Sept., 1895; C.S.I., Jan., 1897; finl. commr., Nov., 1899; addl. member of viceroy's legisl. council, June-Oct., 1900, and Oct., 1902; member of legisl. council of the Punjab, May, 1898, and July, 1900; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903; author of 'Customary Law of the Punjab,' vols. I-III, and of 'Our Indian Protectorate.'

TUPPER, JOHN HOLMAN EYRE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Rugby, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Nov., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

TURAB KHAN, A. (see Abu Turab Khan).

TURING, JULIUS MATHISON, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

TURNBULL, DAVID NISBET, late Punjab Police Dept.—Joined the service, July, 1870, as asst. dist. supt. of police; dist. supt., Jan., 1878; ret'd., 1st May, 1902.

TURNBULL, PETER STEPHENSON, M.D., Surgn.-Major-Gen., late Bombay Med. Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1860, and was employed under the mil. dept. until April, 1864, when he was apptd. supt. of vaccn., central circle; served in the Abyssinian campaign, 1867-68 (medal); examr. of med. accts., Feb., 1876; acted at various periods as supt.-gen. of vaccn., dep. sanitary commr., and inspr.-gen. of prisons; sec. to surgn.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1882; dep. surgn.-gen., Sind, Feb., 1888; surgn.-gen. with the govt. of Bombay, Feb., 1893; ret'd., March, 1896.

TURNBULL, WILLIAM SATOOR, late Bombay Rev. Survey Dept.—Educ. at King's Coll.; joined the service, 14th Aug., 1865, as supernumerary asst. settlt. officer in Sind; asst. supt., rev. survey, Berar, Oct., 1869, and afterwards in

Bombay; dep. supt., Konkan survey, Dec., 1891; supt., Deccan rev. survey, April, 1894; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

TURNER, SIR CHARLES ARTHUR, K.C.I.E., M.A., late member of Council of India.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford (fellow, 1855); called to the bar (Lincoln's Inn), 1858, and went the western circuit; apptd. judge of the high court, N.W. Prov., and arrived, May, 1866; acted as chief justice in 1870, 1875, and 1878; chief justice of the high court, Madras, Jan., 1879; fellow of the univ. of Madras, Jan., 1879; member of the Indian law commn., Bengal, 1879; vice-chancellor of Madras univ., 1880 and 1882; ret'd., July, 1885; member of the publicservice commn., 1885-86; member of the council of India, Feb., 1888-Feb., 1898; vice-prest., 1896-7.

TURNER, CHARLES WILLIAM ALDIS, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Norwich Sch. and Magdalep Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 24th Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

TURNER, EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 20th Nov., 1864, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on famine relief operations, 1877-78; sub-collr. and joint mag., April, 1882; collr. and mag., Cuddapah, Jan., 1885; district and sessions judge, Cuddapah, Feb., 1885; collr. and mag., Madura, Jan., 1886; ret'd., April, 1895.

TURNER, ERNEST GEORGE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 18th Nov., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and mag. and forest settlement offr.

TURNER, ERNEST JAMES, India Office (senior clerk, mil. dept.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, mil. dept., India Office, Oct., 1900; resdt. clerk, April, 1901; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Nov, 1902; senr. clerk, mil. dept., April, 1904.

TURNER, FREDERICK CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 10th Dec., 1895, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.

TURNER, HENRY GRIBBLE, Barr. (1874), late Madras C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1863; asst. collr. and mag., April, 1866; from Aug., 1866, to Dec., 1867, served in the police dept. as asst. supt. and district supt.; postmr.-gen., Madras, March, 1875; district and sessions judge, N. Arcot, June, 1884; collr., &c., at Vizagapatam, Nov., 1885; ret'd., July, 1890.

TURNER, LAWRENCE HERBERT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 18th Dec., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under sec. to govt., May, 1894; joint mag., Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Oct., 1900; mag. and collr., Nov., 1902; dep. commr., 1st grade, Dec., 1903.

TUSON, FRANCIS EDWARD (dep. supt., Port Blair).—Joined the service, 25th Dec., 1868, as extra asst. supt., Port Blair; asst. supt., Dec., 1884; 1st asst. supt., April, 1891; dep. supt., Sept., 1901; offd. as supt., Port Blair, and chief commr. of Andamans and Nicobars, from May, 1903, to April, 1904.

TUTE, RICHARD CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.

TWEEDIE, MAURICE, Col., Indian Army, late Police Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Arrived in India as ensign, 23rd Oct., 1856; during the Mutiny served with the field force in Central India, and subsequently with the mounted police in Oudh, and on the Nepal frontier (medal and clasp); from June, 1860, served as district supt.; junr. dep. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1890; senr. dep., March, 1893; reverted to mil. dept., Sept., 1894; author of a chapter on the fauna of Oudh, for incorporation in the Oudh gazetteer.

TWEEDIE, WILLIAM, C.S.I., Major-Gen., Indian Army.—First commn., Bengal infantry, Jan., 1857; served during the Mutiny (medal and clasps); adjutant, 1st cavalry, Hyderabad contingent, March, 1859; acted as asst. to rest., Hyderabad, from 1863; served in the Abyssinian expedn., 1868 (medal); 1st asst. rest., Hyderabad, and gen. supt., thagi and dakaiti dept., Oct., 1868; agent to govr.-gen., Murshidabad, 1876-77; pol. agent, Gwalior, 1878-79; during the Afghan war was senr. pol. officer with Major-Gen. Bright, during advance on Kabul, and was in charge of Jalalabad divn., March, 1880 (medal, C.S.I.); offg. pol. agent, W. Rajputana states, 1880-81; acted subsequently as rest. in W. Rajputana, in Gwalior, and Turkish Arabia; offg. commr., Ajmir, March-June, 1885; rest. in Turkish Arabia, March, 1886; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1891; author of 'The Arabian Horse,' 1894.

TWEEDY, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian C.S. (addl. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 8th Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and cantonmt. mag.; joint mag., Nov., 1887; asst. commr., March, 1890; supt., Dehra Dun, April, 1891; dep. commr., April, 1894; district and sessions judge, Jan., 1896; mag. and collr., March, 1900; addl. commr., Dec., 1902.

TWEMLOW, EDWARD D'OYLEY, Major-Gen., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—Joined the Indian establ., 9th Dec., 1859; apptd. asst. engr., July, 1862; exec. engr., Dec., 1865; served in Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); transf. to mil. works dept., Sept., 1887; suptg. engr., west of India coast defences, Feb., 1888; transf. to Lahore and Sirhind command, April, 1892; and to Rawal Pindi, Nov., 1892; re-transf. to Lahore and Sirhind, Feb., 1893; sec. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Baluchistan, June, 1895; reverted to mil. dept., Oct., 1895; ret'd., May, 1896.

TWIDELL, JOHN COOK, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at the Oxford High Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 27th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and as sub-divl.

officer; joint. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1900.

TWIGG, JOHN, Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, &c., Madras).—Educ. at Repton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1879, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; special asst., Dec., 1887; head asst., Dec., 1890; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1891; collr. and mag., Oct., 1895; dist. mag. and agent to govt., Ganjam, Feb., 1899, to March, 1900; collr. of customs, protector of emigrants, supt. of stamps and stationery, and chairman, harbour trust board, May, 1903.

TWIGG, JOHN HILL, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1862; arrived, 12th Dec., 1863; from May, 1864, served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; on famine duty, 1874; joint mag. and dep. collr., 1st grade, May, 1880; mag. and collr., Hamirpur, April, 1882; addl. commr., Benares divn., April, 1889; ret'd., July, 1890.

TWISADAY, CHARLES EDWARD JAMES India Office (clerk, Indo-European Telegraph, Public Works Dept.).—Apptd. to the London office of the dept., 29th July, 1869; accompanied the dir.-in-chief to Constantinople in 1880, as personal asst., and to the Persian Gulf in 1885 with the third Persian Gulf cable expedn., and afterwards to India; transfed. to India office, 1st Jan., 1889.

TWOMEY, DANIEL HAROLD RYAN, Barr. (1895), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma).—Educ. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1884, and served in British Burma as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1889; dep. commr., April, 1891; offg. junr. sec., March-July, 1892; offg. sec., March-Sept., 1893, March-Dec., 1896, and Feb., 1898-Jan., 1899; member, legisl. council, March, 1901.

TYABJI, B. (see Badroodin Tyabji).

TYABJI, M. B. (see Mohsin Badrudin Tyabji).

TYLER, HECTOR GRANVILLE SUTHERLAND, M.A., Indian C.S. (pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1901.

TYLER, HENRY HEWEY FRANCIS MACDONALD, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

TYLER, SIR JOHN WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., late Jail Dept., N.W. Prov. and Oudh.—Joined the service as offg. civil surgn., Etah, 6th May, 1863; apptd. also civil surgn. and supt. of jail, Mainpuri, March, 1866; supt., Meerut jails, April, 1873; supt., Agra jails, April, 1875; C.I.E., June, 1886; on special duty in Dholpur state, Dec., 1886; accompanied the Maharaja of Bheartpur to England for H.M.'s Jubilee, 1887; Kt., 1888; inspr.-gen. of prisons, N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Sept., 1890; ret'd., July, 1896.

TYTLER, ADAM GILLIES, C.I.E., late opium dept., Bengal.—Entered the service March, 1862, and served as asst. and sub-dep. opium agent; C.I.E., 1895; ret'd., May, 1901.

UDNY, SIR RICHARD, K.C.S.I., M.A., late

Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Univ. of Aberdeen, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 25th Nov., 1869, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and jud. asst.; dep. commr., Feb., 1885; C.S.I., Jan., 1894; on special duty with Indo-Afghan boundary commn., Aug., 1894; promoted to commr., Dec., 1894; K.C.S.I., June, 1897; served with Tirah expedn. force, Oct., 1897; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

UNDERWOOD, WALTER GREGORY, late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Chester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1868; arrived, 17th Sept., 1870, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on special duty to the Laccadives, Nov., 1881; sub-collr. and joint mag., Dec., 1885; collr. and mag., Jan., 1889; district and sessions judge, Dec., 1894; ret'd., Aug., 1897.

UNWIN, ARTHUR HAMILTON, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Sch., Sherborne, and Tonbridge Gr. Sch.; apptd. after exam. of 1867; arrived, 8th Nov., 1869, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; from Feb., 1873, served in British Burma as asst. commr. and inspr. of schs.; from Jan., 1880, served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, May, 1887; ret'd., Feb., 1896.

UPCOTT, FREDERICK ROBERT, C.S.I., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept. (chairman, railway board, Calcutta).—Joined the service, Dec., 1868, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1874; suptg. engr., Dec., 1890; engr.-in-chief, Bengal-Assam railway, N. section, Nov., 1891; consulting engr. for railways, and joint sec. to govt., railway branch, Madras, Feb., 1893; rank of chief engr., Oct., 1894; offg. dir.-gen. of railways and dep. sec. to govt. of India, May, 1897; dir. of railway construction, Nov., 1897; sec. to govt. of India, Jan., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1900; ret'd. from Public Works Dept., May, 1901; apptd. govt. dir. of guaranteed Indian rly. cos., June, 1901; member of board of visitors, R.I.E. Coll., Jan., 1903; chairman, railway board, Calcutta, 1905.

USBORNE, CHARLES FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 30th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

USSHER, CHRISTOPHER JAMES, Madras Public Works Dept. (suptg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; entered the dept. as asst. engr., Oct., 1877; exec. engr., April, 1890; suptg. engr., Jan., 1904.

VAN GEYZEL, JOHN LAWRENCE, M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service (professor, Madras Med. Coll.).—Joined the service 2nd Oct., 1880; civil surgn., Negapatam, Jan., 1888; chemical examr. and profir. of chemistry, med. coll., Madras, Feb., 1890.

VAN SOMEREN, GODLIEB JAMES, Col., late Forest Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Madras infantry, Sept., 1859; apptd. to the forest dept., May, 1864; conservator, Mysore and Coorg, May, 1869; transfed. to Punjab, 1881; asst. comptr.-gen. of forest accts., Feb., 1883; conservator, Berar, Jan., 1885; also dir. of the forest sch. from Aug., 1889; ret'd., Feb., 1892.

VARADA RAO, TUMALAPALLI, B.A., B.L., Statutory C.S. (sub-judge, Madras).—Educ. at

Madras Univ.; probr. for the civil service, July, 1885; asst. collr. and mag., Dec., 1886; confd. in civil service, 17th Aug., 1887; sub-judge, Mar., 1899.

VARLEY, ALEC WINN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 23rd Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

VARLEY, FREDERICK JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster and at Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., Oct., 1903.

VAS, JOSEPH, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 14th Jan., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VAUGHAN, ERNEST LLEWELLYN, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Blackheath Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Jan., 1888, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and acting asst. acctnt.-gen.; head asst., Nov., 1894; sub-collr. and joint mag., Nov., 1899; collr. and mag., May, 1903.

VAUGHAN, JOHN PERCIVAL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr. in Sind).—Educ. at the Charter-house and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr. in Sind, and sindi translr. to govt., Nov., 1900.

VAUGHAN, JOSEPH CHARLES STOELKE, M.B., Major, Ind. Med. Service (civil surgn., Burdwan).—First commn., 30th Sept., 1889; in military employ till March, 1893; dep. sany. commr., Bengal, Dec., 1895; civil surgn., Burdwan, March, 1900; author of contributions to the medical press.

VAUX, ALFRED ROBERT BRICKWOOD, India Office (asst. editor of codes).—Writer, India office, 29th June, 1874; clerk specially attached, Sept., 1892; resdnt. clerk, May, 1897; asst. editor of codes, Feb., 1903.

VEDARKAR, K. C. (see Khanderao Chimarrao Vedarkar).

VENIS, ARTHUR, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, Queen's Coll., Benares).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; entered the service as acting prof. of English literature, Queen's Coll., Benares, July, 1881; confd., April, 1885; prof. of philosophy, and principal, Sanskrit Coll., Benares, Nov., 1894; principal, Queen's Coll., July, 1897; has translated three Sanskrit works.

VENNING, FREDERICK, late Indian C.S. (Central Prov.).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1863; arrived, 24th Nov., 1864, and served in the Punjab and Central Prov. as asst. commr. and dep. commr.; inspr.-gen. of police and prisons, Central Prov., June, 1880; commr., Aug., 1881; acted also as jud. commr.; ret'd., April, 1894.

VENUGOPAL CHETTI, VENGAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and joint mag., Madras).—Educ. at the Madras Christian Coll., Madras Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 21st Nov., 1890, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. collr. and mag.,

and agent to govr., Vizagapatam, Nov., 1896; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1902.

VERNEDE, ARTHUR HENRY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

VERNON, CHARLES VENABLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 17th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.

VERNON, HAROLD ANSELM BELLAMY, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Clifton and St. John's Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 8th Dec. 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

VERSCHOYLE, JAMES KYNASTON EDWARDS, late India Public Works Dept. —Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Sept., 1881, and posted to Punjab; exec. engr., Feb., 1894; ret'd., Sept., 1900.

VERTUE, WILLIAM, Col., I.S.C., late Central Prov. Commn.—First commn., June, 1857; from 2nd Dec., 1862, served in the police dept. of the Central Prov. as acting district supt., and personal asst. to the inspr.-gen.; from May, 1865, served as asst. commr.; cantonmt. mag., Saugor, Jan., 1877; small cause court judge, Nagpur, Jan., 1882; dep. commr., Sept., 1883; reverted to mil. dept., Nov., 1894.

VIBART, HENRY MEREDITH, Col., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—First commn., Dec., 1857; arrived, 30th Nov., 1859; asst. engr. in the Madras public works dept., Sept., 1861; exec. engr., Dec., 1866; dist. engr., Bangalore, 1872-76; on special duty in Bangalore, 1877-78; in special charge of the restoration of Tirumal Naick's palace, Madura, 1878; acting dep. chief engr. for irrign., and under sec. to govt., public works dept., March, 1879; offg. suptg. engr., Madras, June, 1879; supt. of works, presidency, April, 1880; suptg. engr., April, 1884; ret'd., Dec., 1891; author of 'The Mil. History of the Madras Engrs.,' 'The History of the East India Co.'s Mil. Coll. at Addiscombe,' and 'Richard Baird Smith.'

VIBERT, ARTHUR LIONEL, Indian C.S. (head asst., Madras).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 8th Nov., 1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Dec., 1901.

VIDAL, GEORGE WILLIAM BARR (1877), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 26th Oct., 1867, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. officer; first asst., Dec., 1878; member of the mixed commn. at Goa, 1880 and 1885; prest. of the Bombay forest commn., Dec., 1885; collr. and mag., Panch Mahals, and pol. agent, Rewa Kantha, Sept., 1886; collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Sept., 1888; sec., pol., &c., depts., Feb., 1895; acting chief sec., April, 1895; member of legisl. council from June, 1894; chief sec. pol. dept., July, 1896; ret'd., March, 1897.

VINCENT, CLAUDE, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to Madras; asst. chief engr. and under sec., March-Oct.,

1880; exec. engr., May, 1883; acting private sec. to govr. of Madras, March, 1889; under sec. to govt. of India, civil works branch, Dec., 1889; transf. to Central Prov., 1892; acted as dep. sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., in 1892 and 1893; services lent to govt. of Straits Settlements for Krian irrig. scheme, in 1894; ret'd., Nov., 1898.

VINCENT, HENRY ALEXANDER, Col., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1866; prest., council of regency, Rampur, Nov., 1893; pol. agent, Baghalkhand, and supt., Rewa state, April, 1894-7; pol. agent, Bikanir, April, 1897; res'dt., Bikanir, June, 1897; returned to Europe, June, 1901.

VINCENT, ROBERT WILLIAM EDWARD HAMPE, C.I.E., late Bombay Police Dept.—Joined the service, 1st March, 1869, and served as inspr. of police; asst. supt., May, 1875; supt., March, 1876; commr. of police, Bombay, Sept., 1894; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

VINCENT, WILLIAM HENRY HOARE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal).—Educ. at Christ Coll., Brecon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; mag. and collr., June, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1900.

VISVESVARAIYA, M. (see Mokshagundam Visvesvaraiya).

VOLKERS, ROBERT CHARLES FRANCIS, India Public Works Dept. (examr.).—Apptd. to the dept. as ac-cent., 19th Sept., 1878; asst. examr., Oct., 1882; dep. examr., May, 1886; examr., July, 1894.

VOWELL, CHRISTOPHER HENRY, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag. and dep. collr.; mag. and collr., July, 1884; ret'd., March, 1893.

VYALL, F. W., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd. apprentice engr., 9th June, 1882, and posted to the Punjab (irrign.); transf. to Burma, Nov., 1889; exec. engr., Oct., 1895.

WACE, ALFRED AUGUSTUS, late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1868; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Nov., 1884; in 1891-92 served in Assam as acting judge and commr., Assam valley districts; commr., Bardwan, April, 1894; ret'd., Sept., 1895.

WADDELL, LAWRENCE AUSTINE, C.B., C.I.E., LL.D., M.B., Lieut.-Col., Indian Med. Service.—Joined the service, 31st March, 1880; offg. civil surg., Jhelum, April, 1881; civil surg., Meerut, 1882; rest. physician, med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Feb., 1883; profir. of pathology, 1884; supt. of vaccn. and dep. sanitary commr., March, 1885; served through the Burmese war, 1886-87; archaeologist with the Chitral Relief Force, 1895; chemical examr. to govt. and profir. of chemistry, med. coll., Calcutta, Jan., 1896; in medl. charge No. 1 native hosp. China expedy. force, Aug., 1900; P.M.O., Northern China, July, 1901; C.I.E., July, 1901; served with Tibet Mission, 1904; C.B., Dec., 1904; author of 'The Buddhism of Tibet,' &c., &c.

WADDINGTON, C. W., C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educ. Service (principal, Mayo coll.).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. vice-principal, Rajkumar coll., Rajkot, May, 1892; principal, Feb., 1896; principal, Mayo College, Ajmer, March, 1903; C.I.E., June, 1904.

WAGLE, KRISHNAJI BALWANT, M.A. (asst. ac-cent.-genl., Bombay).—Entered finance dept., Bombay, Feb., 1886; transferred to N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Mar., 1893; to Burma, Mar., 1896; asst. ac-cent. gen., Dec., 1898; dep.-ac-cent.-genl., Bombay, April, 1901; asst. ac-cent.-gen., Bombay, June, 1904.

WAHAB, ROBERT ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Bt.-Col., R.E. (supt., Survey of India Dept.).—First commn., 29th April, 1873; asst. supt., survey dept., Jan., 1883; dep. supt., July, 1888, and supt., Dec., 1896; served in the Afghan war, 1879-80, in Mahsud Waziri expdn., 1881, Zhob valley expdn., 1884, and with Hazara field force and Miranzai field force in 1888 and 1891; C.I.E., June, 1897; on deputation with Aden Boundary Delimitation Commn., 1902.

WAHL, BRUNO WOLFGANG, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Friedrich Wilhelm Gym., Cologne, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.

WAIT, LEWIS GORHAM, late India Postal Dept.—Joined the service, 28th May, 1870; asst. dir.-gen., foreign post branch, Calcutta, Dec., 1880; first asst. dir.-gen. of post office, Sept., 1890; dep. postmr.-gen., Sind and Baluchistan, April, 1892; ret., Aug., 1901.

WAKELY, LEONARD DAY, India Office (private sec. to parly. under sec. of state).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. junior clerk, judl. and public dept., 15th Oct., 1902; res'dt. clerk, Nov., 1902; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, April, 1904.

WALCH, GEORGE TURNER, M.I.C.E., late Madras Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 31st March, 1866; exec. engr., April, 1873; suptg. engr., March, 1887; chief engr. for irrign., Sept., 1889; joint sec., public works dept., with charge of irrign. branch, July, 1890; author of 'Notes on Some of the Navigable Rivers and Canals of the United States and Canada,' 1876; ret'd., April, 1893.

WALES, JOHN ADOLPHUS GATGENS, B.A., Indian C.S. (2nd asst., Bombay).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 11th Dec., 1894, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; admr., Sachin state, May, 1898; 2nd asst., April, 1901.

WALKDEN, ERLE THOMAS CAMPBELL, India Office (second-class clerk, military dept.).—Educ. at Rochester Mathl. Sch.; served in S. African war, 1901-2 (medal and five clasps); apptd. 2nd class clerk, 20th Sept., 1902.

WALKER, ALEXANDER COULL, Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 30th Oct., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1899.

WALKER, GEORGE CASSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. sec. to the Nizam).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after 21 n.

of 1875; arrived, 6th Jan., 1878, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; from April, 1883, served in the finl. dept. of the govt. of India, and was apptd. asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab; transf. to office of acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1883; from Feb., 1884, served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and offg. district judge; served under the govt. of India from Sept. to Nov., 1886, as under sec., fin. and commerce dept.; settl. officer, Lahore, Feb., 1888; also offg. dir. of land records and agric. in 1892, and offg. commr. of excise, &c., 1894; commr. of excise, &c., Nov., 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1893; as depn. as finl. sec. to the Nizam, from Nov., 1901.

WALKER, GEORGE HERBERT DACRES, C.I.E., B.A., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. sec. to govt. of N.W. Prov. and Oudh, 1st May, 1874; served subsequently in accts. branch; under sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., May, 1887; C.I.E., Nov., 1901; ret'd., Oct., 1903.

WALKER, GEORGE WARREN, Gen., R.E., late Public Works Dept., Madras.—Second lieut., Madras engrs., 9th Dec., 1842; asst. civil engr., 1847; district engr., Kanara, Sept., 1859; suptg. engr., Coimbatore, May, 1863; chief engr., and sec. to govt. of Madras, public works dept., May, 1872; ret'd. from the dept. in 1876; placed on unemployed supy. list, Feb., 1882.

WALKER, GILBERT THOMAS, M.A. (meteorological reporter).—Apptd. special scientific asst. to the meteorological reporter to the govt. of India, 15th Dec., 1902; meteorological reporter and dir.-gen. of Indian observatories, Jan., 1904.

WALKER, HARRY BRUCE, India Office (supervisor, Store Dépôt).—Matriculated at London Univ., 1875; clerk, 2nd class, India store Dépôt, 6th July, 1877; 2nd grade supervisor, July, 1888; 1st grade ditto, Feb., 1904.

WALKER, JAMES, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Prov.).—Educ. at the Univ. of Aberdeen and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 1st Dec., 1886, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Central Prov. as asst. commr., Dec. 1890; dep. commr., Jan., 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1904.

WALKER, JAMES WILLIAM, LL.B., Barr. (1868), late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1866; arrived, 5th Dec., 1868, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; acting rememberer of legal affairs, 1880; judge and sessions judge, Feb., 1885; ret'd., Nov., 1893.

WALKER, THOMAS GORDON, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab).—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and Univ. of Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 4th Nov., 1872, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and was settl. officer of Ludhiana, 1878-84; also acted as under sec. to govt., registrar of chief court, &c.; senr. sec. to finl. commr., Feb., 1888; commr. of excise, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Nov., 1888; dep. commr., April, 1896; divl. judge, Jan., 1898; judge, chief court, Dec., 1898; commr. and supt., March, 1902; C.S.I., June, 1903.

WALKER, THORNTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sessions judge, Bombay).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 8th Dec., 1885,

and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and 2nd asst.; judge and sessions judge, April, 1897.

WALKLATE, HAROLD THORNTON, India Office (second class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, India audit office, 31st Oct., 1892; transf. to record and registry dept., India office, 9th Aug., 1894.

WALLACE, ALFRED CHARLES, India Office (second class clerk, Acctnt.-Gen.'s Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, 17th Feb., 1872.

WALLACE, SIR DONALD MACKENZIE, K.C.I.E., M.A., LL.D., late private sec. to Viceroy of India.—Educ. at the Univs. of Edinburgh, Heidelberg, and Berlin, and the Ecole de droit, Paris; was 'Times' correspondent in Russia, Austria, and the East, 1878-84; private sec. to the govr.-gen. of India (Lord Dufferin), 1884-85; member of mission to Kabul, 1888; accompanied the Czarewitch during his visit to India, 1890-91; author of 'Russia,' 'Egypt,' and the Egyptian Question,' &c.

WALLACE, EDWARD HAMILTON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 16th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WALLACE, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1877, and posted to state railways as asst. engr.; transf. to Burma, March, 1883; exec. engr., Nov., 1889; re-transf. to state railways, Oct., 1893; ret'd., Feb., 1902.

WALLER, DAVID GRIERSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bombay, April, 1901; re-transf. to Madras, Dec., 1901.

WALLER, EDMUND, Major, Indian Army, (cantonmt. mag., Punjab).—First commn., 7th Feb., 1885; in mil. employ till June, 1891, since when he has held appt. of cantonmt. mag. at various stations; St. Thomas' Mount, Jan., 1903; Ferozepore, June, 1904.

WALLER, EDMUND AUGUSTUS, Lieut.-Col., R.E., India Public Works Dept. (dep. acctnt.-gen.).—First commn., April, 1873; apptd. to the dept. as asst. engr., Nov., 1876; served in Afghan war of 1879-80 (medal); apptd. to accts. estabt., Sept., 1884, as dep. examr.; examr., May, 1886; dep. acctnt.-gen., Mar., 1899; ditto, and under sec. to govt. of India, Aug., 1901.

WALLER, RICHARD MAUNSELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1865; arrived, 4th Dec., 1867, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1878; dep. commr., April, 1879; mag. and collr., Aug., 1885; ret'd., Jan., 1895.

WALLIS, BERESFORD GAHAN, L.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 17th July, 1871, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1881; suptg. engr., July, 1897; has received Afghan medal, 1879-80, and Punjab Frontier, 1897-98, medal and clasp; ret'd., Jan., 1904.

WALLIS, JOHN EDWARD POWER, M.A. (advocate-general, Madras).—Called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1886; apptd. advocate-general

at Madras, Mar., 1900; has edited several volumes of the State Trials.

WALMSLEY, HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 28th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1902.

WALPOLE, SIR HORACE GEORGE, K.C.B. (asst. under sec. of state, India Office).—Educ. at Eton; temporary clerk, India office, 12th Dec., 1862; junr. clerk, June, 1863; store dept., 1863; acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 1863-64; public works dept., 1864; rest. clerk, 1870-77; senr. clerk, April, 1877; private sec. to the permanent under sec. of state (Mr. Herman Merivale, C.B.), 1866-74, and to the sec. of state (the Marquis of Salisbury, 1874-78, and Viscount Cranbrook, 1878-80); C.B., 1880; also served as clerk of the council from 1877, and was apptd. asst. to the under secs. of state in addn. to his other duties, Sept., 1881; asst. under sec. of state, Nov., 1883, continuing to be clerk of the council; royal commr. for the Colonial and Indian exhibn., 1886; sec. to the royal commn. on civil establishs., 1886-90; K.C.B., Aug., 1892; chairman of committee on reorganisation of educn. and science and art depts., 1890.

WALSH, ERNEST HERBERT COOPER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal).—Educ. at Trent Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd., after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1891; mag. and collr., Sept., 1895; junr. sec., board of rev., June, 1896; dep. commr., Aug., 1901.

WALSH, ERNST PAKENHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Madras).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.

WALSH, JOHN HENRY TULL, Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (supt., Campbell med. sch. and hosp., Calcutta).—First commn., 1st April, 1884; in mil. employ till 11th June, 1888; and rest. surgn., presy. gen. hosp., Calcutta, March, 1890; 1st ditto, March, 1892; civil surgn., Bengal, April, 1896; supt., Campbell med. sch. and hosp., Calcutta, Oct., 1903; author of numerous contributions to the medical press, and editor of 7th edition of 'Moore's Family Medicine.'

WALSH, LANGTON PRENDERGAST, C.I.E., late Bombay Pol. Dept.—Entered the service as asst. mail offr., April, 1873; British agent and vice-consul, Berbera, Aug., 1884; asst. pol. rest., Aden, Oct., 1885; C.I.E., Jan., 1889; asst. pol. rest., Belgaum, May, 1893; pol. supt., Savantvadi, Feb., 1897; 1st asst., pol. agent, Kathiawar, Feb., 1901; pol. agent, Savantvadi, Nov., 1901; ret'd., May, 1903.

WALTON, ELLYS WILLIAM, Major, R.E., India Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.).—Joined the dept., 18th Feb., 1885, as asst. engr., and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Sept., 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Calcutta, Jan., 1895; ditto, Burma, March, 1897, to Jan., 1899.

WALTON, FREDERICK THOMAS GRANVILLE, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. to dept. as engr.-in-chief, Oudh and Rohilkhand railway, 1st Jan., 1889; ret'd.

WALTON, HENRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Prov.).—

Educ. at Newcastle Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.

WAPSHARE, ARTHUR, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army.—First commn., 28th Oct., 1871; served in the Soudan expedn., 1884-85 (medal, clasp and star); offg. cantonmt. mag., Karachi, 1887-88; cantonmt. mag., Deesa, Sept., 1888; ret'd., Nov., 1903.

WARBURTON, HARRIS GRANT, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Dover Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 5th Dec., 1887, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; from Oct., 1891, to Aug., 1893, was employed under the govt. of India as asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, and asst. compt.-gen. (paper currency); joint mag., March, 1895; dep. commr., March, 1899; mag. and collr., July, 1900.

WARBURTON, JOHN PAUL, late Police Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service, 28th May, 1864, as asst. district supt. of police; district supt., April, 1873; asst. inspr.-gen., railway police, June, 1894; ret'd., 1900.

WARBURTON, PHILIP BANNERMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 18th Nov., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.

WARBURTON, WILLIAM PLEACE, C.S.I., M.D., Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the Indian med. dept., 31st March, 1866; civil surgn., Jalandhar, April, 1868; acted as supt. of central jail, Lahore, 1872-73; med. officer to the Maharaja of Kapurthala, June, 1874; principal med. officer and sanitary commr., Assam, Jan., 1894; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, April, 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; ret'd., Jan., 1899.

WARD, ALFRED WILLIAM, India Office (2nd class clerk, Registry and Record Dept.).—Clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 2nd Aug., 1892; transfid. to registry and record dept., Dec., 1894; acted as sec. to Imperial Yeomanry field hospital, S. African War, 1899-1900 (medal with 3 clasps).

WARD, FRANCIS WYNDHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WARD, GEORGE ERNEST, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (N.W. Prov. and Oudh).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1860; arrived, 31st Oct., 1861, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. supt. of Dehra Dun, and joint mag.; mag. and collr., Jaunpur, July, 1879; commr., Jhansi divn., July, 1885; ret'd., July, 1889.

WARD, HENRY CONSTANTINE EVELYN, C.I.E., Col., Indian Army, late Central Prov. Commn.—Arrived as ensign, April, 1855; was employed under the mil. dept. up to 1864, including service in the Mutiny (medal and clasp), the Yusufzai expedn., 1858, and the campaign against the Mahsud Waziris, 1859-60 (frontier medal); asst. commr., Central Prov., Dec., 1864; dep. commr.,

May, 1870; acting inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Nov., 1873; addl. commr. in Kalahandi, June, 1882; special commr. for the repression of dakaiti in Narbada divn., Feb., 1886; from June, 1882, served under the govt. of India, as minister of the Bhopal state; commr., Narbada divn., Aug., 1889; reverted to mil. dept., 1892.

WARD, THEODORE METHUEN, Col., late Survey Dept., Sind.—Joined the service, 9th Dec., 1859; lieut., Bombay artillery, April, 1860; admitted to the rev. survey dept., Nov., 1864; supt., Sind rev. survey, June, 1889; reverted to mil. duty, 1890; ret'd., Dec., 1891.

WARD, THOMAS ROBERT JOHN, India Public Works dept. (exec. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 23rd Sept., 1883, and posted to the Punjab; exec. engr., April, 1896; on depn. with Seistan boundary commn. from Nov., 1902.

WARD, SIR WILLIAM ERSKINE, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1859; arrived, 29th Nov., 1861, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and joint mag.; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., 1865-66, and 1866-67; under sec. in dept. of rev., agric., and commerce, March, 1873; from Oct., 1873, served in jud. dept. as acting district and sessions judge; transf. to Assam, March, 1876, and served as judge and commr. of Assam valley districts; offd. temporarily as jud. commr. of Central Prov., 1883; fellow of univ. of Calcutta; offg. chief commr. of Assam, July-Oct., 1883, and from Feb., 1885, to Oct., 1887; offg. jud. commr., British Burma, April, 1884, to Feb., 1885; confd. as jud. commr., Burma, May, 1886, continuing to act as chief commr. of Assam; C.S.I., Jan., 1888; took charge of substantive appt. of jud. commr. of Burma, March, 1889; chief commr. of Assam, May, 1891; K.C.S.I., May, 1896; ret'd., Nov., 1896.

WARD-SMITH, WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Thomason Coll.; apptd., 3rd Dec., 1872, and employed on irrign. works in N.W. Prov. and Oudh; exec. engr., Dec, 1881; ret'd., Oct., 1901.

WARDE, HUGH NORTH, Major, Indian Army (dep. commr., Burma).—First commn., 6th Feb., 1884; apptd. to the Burma commn., Dec., 1886, and served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., 1900.

WARDEN, AUGUSTUS BROOKE, late Bombay C.S.—Educ. at the Kensington Gr. Sch., and Haileybury Coll.; apptd., 1842, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; judge and sessions judge, March, 1856; judge of the high court, 1865; was also prest. of the deptl. exams. committee for junr. civilians for five years; ret'd., 1871.

WARDEN, FREDERICK, Commander, late R.I.M.—Joined the Indian navy, 2nd March, 1852; from Dec., 1865, served as port-master of Canning, and asst. collr. of customs; from Dec., 1866, acted as asst. master attendant, and agent for govt. consignments, Calcutta, and from March, 1870, as agent for transports; also govt. prosecutor for trial of pilots, May, 1873, to March, 1876; asst. port officer, Aug., 1879; examr. in navigation, Nov., 1879; transf. to Rangoon as port officer, &c., March, 1882; ret'd., Sept., 1890.

WARDEN, HOWARD WILLIAM, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., 8th June, 1871, and posted to state rlys.; asst. manager, Rajputana rly., Dec., 1875; exec. engr., Jan., 1880; asst. dir. of state rlys., N.E. system and asst. sec. to govt. of Bengal, Dec., 1880; manager, Nizam's rly., May, 1883; dep. consg. engr. and under-sec. to govt. of Madras, April, 1886; ditto, ditto, Bombay, July, 1887; consg. engr., Assam, Nov., 1892; ret'd., Jan., 1894.

WARDEN, JOHN LODWICK, B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. (1865), late Jud. Dept. Bombay.—Arrived, 8th March, 1852, and served in the Bombay civ. civil service as asst. collr. and mag., and dep. mag. in charge of the adalat, Hyderabad; resigned the civil service, May, 1858; apptd. extra asst. sec. to govt. of Bombay, jud. dept., Dec., 1866; asst. judge, April, 1867; from Jan., 1869, served as small cause court judge, and was apptd. third judge, Bombay, Dec., 1874, and second judge, May, 1888; ret'd., 1889.

WARING, MICHAEL LOUIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 24th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; registrar, chief court, Punjab, Dec., 1899, to April, 1902.

WARRY, WILLIAM, M.A., late Burma Pol. Dept.—Educ. at Exeter Coll., Oxford; in 1878 passed the competitive exam. for student interpreterships in China; was acting asst. Chinese sec. at Peking from Oct., 1881 to July, 1882; employed on special service under the govt. of India from Dec., 1885; received the Burma war medal for active service, 1887-88; apptd. pol. officer on special duty in Burma, 10th Feb., 1887; adviser on Chinese affairs, April, 1892; on depn. with Burma-China boundary commn., Nov., 1897, to April, 1898; ret'd., Nov., 1904; author of a transln. of the Peking Gazette, 1881-82.

WATERFIELD, BERTRAM CLARKE, Capt., Indian Army (dep. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.).—First commn., 19th Sept., 1888; in mil. employ till June, 1892, when he was apptd. boundary settlmt. offr., Bhopwar; asst. commr., Punjab, April, 1893; ditto, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1903.

WATERFIELD, EDWARD HOPE, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. and mag., Bombay).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent.

WATERFIELD, FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Punjab).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

WATERFIELD, SIR HENRY, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B., late Finl. Dept. India Office.—Educ. at Westminster; junr. clerk, India board, 1st Sept., 1853; finl. and railway dept., 1853; pol. and secret dept., 1856; private sec. to prest. of the India board, 1858; India office, finl. dept., 1858; asst. private sec. and précis writer to the sec. of state, 1860; finl. dept., 1863; senr. clerk, April, 1863; asst. sec.

in the mil. dept., 1867; public works dept., 1871; sec. in the statistics and commerce dept., 1874; sec., finl. dept., 1879; C.B., Dec., 1885; sec. of the Indian currency committee, 1892-93; K.C.S.I., July, 1893; G.C.I.E., June, 1902; ret'd., July, 1902; author of the first report on the moral and material progress of India, 1866, and of a memo. on the census of British India of 1871-72, both of which were presented to parliament.

WATERFIELD, STUART SHAKSPEAR, Punjab Commn. (pol. agent, Kurram, N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1837, as offg. asst. dist. supt. of police, Punjab; confd., Feb., 1891; cmdt., border mil. police, Oct., 1893; served with Chitral Relief force, 1895 and 1896; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1902; pol. agent, Kurram, Dec., 1902.

WATERFIELD, WILLIAM, late Bengal C.S.—Educ. at Westminster; apptd. from Haileybury, 1852; arrived, 10th Nov., 1852; served in Bengal in the rev. and survey depts. till 1859, when he was transf. to the finl. dept. as 1st asst. to the acctnt.-gen. for India; acted as sub-treasurer, Madras, and as civil auditor and supt. of stamps, Madras, Calcutta, and Bombay; from 1864 to 1877 served successively as civil pay-master, dep. acctnt. - gen., and acctnt. - gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh; comptr.-gen., 1877-80; ret'd., 1881.

WATERHOUSE, JAMES, Major-Gen., Indian Army, late Survey of India Dept.—Lieut., Bengal artillery, June, 1859; arrived 11th Oct., 1859; asst. rev. surveyor, Lower Prov., June, 1866; asst. surveyor with superintendence of the photoincographic operations in the office of the surveyor-gen. of India, July, 1866; asst. supt. of survey, June, 1870; on special duty with the Indian eclipse expedn., 1871-72, and for observing the transit of Venus, 1874-75; in charge of Indian eclipse expedn. to Nicobar Islands, 1875; asst. surveyor-gen., April, 1874; dep. supt., and asst. surveyor-gen., July, 1882; supt. (new scale), and asst. surveyor-gen., Aug., 1892; in charge of photolithographic office, Calcutta, from Dec., 1886; returned to Europe, June, 1897; author of a pamphlet on 'The Application of Photography to the Reproduction of Maps and Plans,' and other reports and handbooks on professional subjects.

WATERS, GEORGE, L.R.C.S., L.R.C.P. (Ed.), Lieut.-Col., late Indian Med. Service.—Joined the Bombay med. service, 1st Oct., 1859; arrived, 27th May, 1870, and served in the mil. dept., and as acting ophthalmic surgn., Bombay, and civil surgn. at Bussire; surgn. to govr. of Bombay, May, 1877; from Oct., 1881, served as physician and registrar, Jamsetji Jijibhoj hosp., curator of the pathological museum, and profr., Grant med. coll.; presidency surgn., 3rd district, Nov., 1889; ret'd., April, 1901.

WATKINS, LAWRENCE AUGUSTUS, late Jud. Dept., Bombay.—Arrived, 10th Dec., 1863; served in the dept. from Jan., 1865, as dep. registrar of the high court, clerk to the high court judges, and commr. for taking affidavits; ret'd., Oct., 1898.

WATSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S., (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., Magdalen Coll., Cambridge,

and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 21st Dec., 1892, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

WATSON, CHARLES CUNINGHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. to agent in Rajputana).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Ch. Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 26th Nov., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to governor's agent in Kathiawar, Oct., 1902; asst. commr. in Merwara, May, 1903; asst. to govr.-gen'l's. agent in Rajputana, March, 1904.

WATSON, CHARLES JAMES KNIGHT, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1874, and posted to Bengal; on famine duty in Madras, 1877-78; exec. engr., May, 1883; employed in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as examr., public works accts., 1888-89, and in Madras, 1889-92; transf. to Burma, Feb., 1892; ret'd., Feb., 1901.

WATSON, GEORGE HERBERT, Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (cantonmt. mag., United Prov.).—Joined the service 11th Sept., 1876; in mil. employ till June, 1883; offd. as cantonmt. mag. in the United Prov. from June, 1883, to Sept., 1886; confd. in that appt., Feb., 1888, since when he has served in that capacity in various stations.

WATSON, HUBERT DIGBY, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr. N.W. Frontier Prov.).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1893, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., May, 1898; pol. offr., Wana, Jan., 1899; asst. commr., Oct., 1900; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1904.

WATSON, SIR JOHN, G.C.B., V.C., Gen., Indian Army, late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., Bombay infantry, 1848; served in Punjab campaign, 1848-49, Bozdar expedn., 1857, Mutiny, 1857-58, and Ambela expedn., 1863; from Sept., 1871, served as commandant, Central India horse, and pol. agent; rest. and agent to the govr.-gen. at Gwalior, April, 1877, also acting as agent for Central India; brigadier-gen. commanding cavalry brigade, Malta expedny. force, 1878; commanding Punjab chiefs' contingent in Afghanistan, 1878-79; in pol. charge of the district of Kuram, 1879-80; rest., and agent to the govr.-gen., Baroda, Jan., 1882; ret'd. from the dept., March, 1886.

WATT, SIR GEORGE, Kt., C.I.E., M.B., Rev. and Agric. Dept., Govt. of India (reporter on economic products).—Apptd., 11th July, 1873; arrived, 4th Nov., 1873; served in the Bengal educn. dept. as profr., presidency, Hugli and Patna colls.; on special duty in connection with the Burma-Manipur boundary expedn., 1881, and for Calcutta international exhibn., 1883; served under govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., from May, 1884; apptd. to charge of imperial Indian economic court of Colonial and Indian exhibn.; C.I.E., June, 1886; reporter on economic products, April, 1887; Kt., Jan., 1903; author of several scientific papers, and editor of the 'Dictionary of Indian Economic Products.'

WATTS, GEORGE KEMPTHORNE, A.M.I.C.E., India Public Works Dept. (supfg. engr.).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1875, and posted to Hyderabad; exec. engr.,

Nov., 1881; asst. to supt. engr., and asst. sec. to rest., Sept., 1886; transfid. to Assam, 1895; to N.W. Provs. and Oudh, Dec., 1900; supt. engr., Dec., 1900.

WAY, ROBERT ATWELL, M.I.C.E., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at Cooper's Hill Coll.; apptd., 1st Oct., 1873, and posted to state railways, exec. engr., Oct., 1880; services lent to Bengal and N.W. railway co., 1884; to Bengal-Nagpur co., 1887; and to Delhi-Ambala-Kalka co., 1889; employed on Chittagong-Akyab-Minhlra railway survey, Oct., 1893; supt. engr., Aug., 1895; ret'd., 1899.

WAY, THEODORE ALBAN HENRY, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Prov.).—Educ. at Portsmouth Gr. Sch., Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 26th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Jan., 1896; joint mag., Mar., 1896; dep. commr., Nov., 1903.
